revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「{{博物館|名称=宮崎県立西都原考古博物館|native_name=The Saitobaru Archeological Museum of Miyazaki Prefecture|native_name_lang=en|imagesize…」
revision.text:
{{博物館|名称=宮崎県立西都原考古博物館|native_name=The Saitobaru Archeological Museum of Miyazaki Prefecture|native_name_lang=en|imagesize=280px|画像=File:Saitobaru Archaeology Museum.jpg|画像説明=正面入り口 {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord2|32|07|31.3|N|131|22|56.6|E}}}}|正式名称=宮崎県立西都原考古博物館|愛称=|前身=西都原資料館|専門分野=歴史学・考古学・西都原古墳群|収蔵作品数=|来館者数=|館長=|学芸員=|研究職員=|事業主体=[[宮崎県]]|管理運営=|年運営費=|建物設計=鉄筋コンクリート造4階建て（地下1階、地上3階）|延床面積=6,678.63平方メートル|開館=2004年（平成16年）4月17日|閉館=|所在地郵便番号=881-0005|所在地=宮崎県西都市大字三宅字西都原西5670番|緯度度=32|緯度分=07|緯度秒=31.3|経度度=131|経度分=22|経度秒=56.6|アクセス=|URL=[http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/index.html 公式サイト]}} '''宮崎県立西都原考古博物館'''（みやざきけんりつさいとばるこうこはくぶつかん）は、[[宮崎県]][[西都市]][[大字]]三宅[[小字|字]]西都原西5670に所在する[[考古学]]系[[博物館]]。国の[[特別史跡]]・[[西都原古墳群]]そばにあり、当古墳群についての展示をメインに、宮崎県内の[[遺跡]]や[[考古学]]についての催しを行う。
== 概要 == 宮崎県の中央部、西都原台地に広がる西都原古墳群は、[[陵墓参考地]]の[[男狭穂塚古墳]]・[[女狭穂塚古墳]]を含めて319基が造営された巨大[[古墳群]]である<ref>{{Cite web|title=宮崎県立西都原考古博物館｜西都原古墳群の概要|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/guidance.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>。西都原考古博物館は、当古墳群の[[考古資料]]を収蔵・保管・展示するほか、[[古墳時代]]や他の時代の様々な[[考古学]]系テーマを取り上げ公開していくことをコンセプトとして、2004年（平成16年）4月17日に開館した<ref>{{Cite web|title=宮崎県立西都原考古博物館｜企画展アーカイブ|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/exhibition.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>。
=== 特徴 === 日本列島全般の古墳時代についての展示のほか、 [[南九州]]に特徴的な[[地下式横穴墓]]を含めた西都原古墳群についての展示、[[律令制|律令期]]（[[飛鳥時代|飛鳥]]・[[奈良時代]]）に[[化外の地|化外の民]]として扱われた[[隼人]]についての展示も行っている。年に数回の企画展示のほか、考古学系講座や講演会・体験イベントを実施する。
「常設展示」という概念を廃し、常に新しい情報を展示・提供する、として「常新展示」という概念を導入している{{Sfn|北郷|2004|pp=1-2}}。また遺跡から出土した資料に直接触れて鑑賞し、体感的に学ぶことを目的とした「ハンズオン展示」や、館内展示・設備の位置などを造形的に示した立体サイン（触察ピクト）を設置し、視覚・聴覚障害者にも利用し易く、また来館者自らが主体的に学べることを目的とした[[ユニバーサルデザイン]]を導入している<ref>{{Cite web|title=宮崎県立西都原考古博物館｜ユニバーサルデザイン|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/universal.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>。そのため、展示室には基本的に順路や導線が存在しない。
== 付属施設 == [[ファイル:Saitobaru-kofungun_yokoana-bogun.JPG|サムネイル|280x280ピクセル|酒元ノ上[[横穴墓]]群遺構保存覆屋（2013年8月24日）]] [[ファイル:Saitobaru_Konohanakan.jpg|サムネイル|280x280ピクセル|このはな館（2019年1月4日）]]
* 古代生活体験館 * 西都原ガイダンスセンター「このはな館」 * 酒元ノ上[[横穴墓]]群遺構保存覆屋 * 西都原111号墳・4号[[地下式横穴墓]]保存見学施設
== 利用案内 ==
* 入場料：無料 * 開館時間：午前10時から午後6時まで * 休館日：月曜日・祝日の翌日・年末年始（12月28日から翌1月4日まで） * 駐車場：普通車100台、大型バス8台、車椅子専用駐車場8台<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=宮崎県立西都原考古博物館｜利用案内|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/usage.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>
== アクセス ==
* バス：[[宮交シティ]]より「西都バスセンター（西都原考古博物館前経由）」行き乗車（約70分）。「西都原考古博物館前」下車 * 自動車：[[宮崎市]]から[[国道219号線]]約40分。[[東九州自動車道]][[西都インターチェンジ]]約10分<ref name=":0" />
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 ==
* {{Cite journal|和書|last=本郷|first=泰道|year=2004-06-30|date=2004-06-30|title=常設展示をなくそう|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/collumn/007.pdf|journal=西都原考古博物館コラム|volume=7|pages=1-12|publisher=西都原考古博物館|ref=harv}}
== 関連項目 ==
* [[西都原古墳群]]
== 外部リンク ==
* [https://www.saito-kanko.jp/sightseeing/%e5%ae%ae%e5%b4%8e%e7%9c%8c%e7%ab%8b%e8%a5%bf%e9%83%bd%e5%8e%9f%e8%80%83%e5%8f%a4%e5%8d%9a%e7%89%a9%e9%a4%a8 一般社団法人・西都市観光協会] * [http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/index.html 公式サイト]
{{DEFAULTSORT:みやさきけんりつさいとはるこうこはくふつかん}} {{考古学}} {{博物館法の博物館}} [[Category:2004年開設の博物館]]

{{博物館 |名称=宮崎県立西都原考古博物館 |native_name=The Saitobaru Archeological Museum of Miyazaki Prefecture |native_name_lang=en |imagesize=280px |画像=File:Saitobaru Archaeology Museum.jpg |画像説明=正面入り口 {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord2|32|07|31.3|N|131|22|56.6|E}}}} |正式名称=宮崎県立西都原考古博物館 |愛称= |前身=西都原資料館 |専門分野=歴史学・考古学・西都原古墳群 |収蔵作品数= |来館者数= |館長= |学芸員= |研究職員= |事業主体=[[宮崎県]] |管理運営= |年運営費= |建物設計=鉄筋コンクリート造4階建て（地下1階、地上3階） |延床面積=6,678.63m<sup>2</sup> |開館=2004年（平成16年）4月17日 |閉館= |所在地郵便番号=881-0005 |所在地=宮崎県西都市大字三宅字西都原西5670番 |緯度度=32|緯度分=07|緯度秒=31.3 |経度度=131|経度分=22|経度秒=56.6 |アクセス= |URL=[http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/index.html 公式サイト] }} '''宮崎県立西都原考古博物館'''（みやざきけんりつ さいとばるこうこはくぶつかん）は、[[宮崎県]][[西都市]][[大字]]三宅[[小字|字]]西都原西に所在する[[考古学]]系[[博物館]]。国の[[特別史跡]]・[[西都原古墳群]]そばにあり、同古墳群についての展示を主とし、宮崎県内の[[遺跡]]や[[考古学]]についての催しを行う。

== 利用案内 == * 入場料：無料 * 開館時間：午前10時から午後6時まで * 休館日：月曜日・祝日の翌日・年末年始（12月28日から翌1月4日まで） * 駐車場：普通車100台、大型バス8台、車椅子専用駐車場8台<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=宮崎県立西都原考古博物館｜利用案内|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/usage.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>
== アクセス == * バス：[[宮交シティ]]より「西都バスセンター（西都原考古博物館前経由）」行き乗車（約70分）。「西都原考古博物館前」下車 * 自動車：[[宮崎市]]から[[国道219号線]]約40分。[[東九州自動車道]][[西都インターチェンジ]]約10分<ref name=":0" />
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite journal|和書|last=北郷|first=泰道|year=2004|date=2004-06-30|title=常設展示をなくそう|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/collumn/007.pdf|journal=西都原考古博物館コラム|volume=7|pages=1-2|publisher=西都原考古博物館|format=PDF|ref=harv}}
== 関連項目 == * [[西都原古墳群]]
== 外部リンク == * [https://www.saito-kanko.jp/sightseeing/%e5%ae%ae%e5%b4%8e%e7%9c%8c%e7%ab%8b%e8%a5%bf%e9%83%bd%e5%8e%9f%e8%80%83%e5%8f%a4%e5%8d%9a%e7%89%a9%e9%a4%a8 一般社団法人・西都市観光協会] * [http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/index.html 公式サイト]
{{考古学}} {{博物館法の博物館}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みやさきけんりつさいとはるこうこはくふつかん}} [[Category:日本の考古博物館]] [[Category:日本の公立博物館]] [[Category:宮崎県の博物館]] [[Category:2004年開設の博物館]] [[Category:西都市の教育]] [[Category:西都市の建築物]] [[Category:西都市の歴史]]

{{博物館 |名称=宮崎県立西都原考古博物館 |native_name=The Saitobaru Archeological Museum of Miyazaki Prefecture |native_name_lang=en |imagesize=280px |画像=File:Saitobaru Archaeology Museum.jpg |画像説明=正面入り口 {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord2|32|07|31.3|N|131|22|56.6|E}}}} |正式名称=宮崎県立西都原考古博物館 |愛称= |前身=西都原資料館 |専門分野=歴史学・考古学・西都原古墳群 |収蔵作品数= |来館者数= |館長= |学芸員= |研究職員= |事業主体=[[宮崎県]] |管理運営= |年運営費= |建物設計=鉄筋コンクリート造4階建て（地下1階、地上3階） |延床面積=6,678.63m<sup>2</sup> |開館=2004年（平成16年）4月17日 |閉館= |所在地郵便番号=881-0005 |所在地=宮崎県西都市大字三宅字西都原西5670番 |緯度度=32|緯度分=07|緯度秒=31.3 |経度度=131|経度分=22|経度秒=56.6 |アクセス= |URL=[http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/index.html 公式サイト] }} '''宮崎県立西都原考古博物館'''（みやざきけんりつさいとばるこうこはくぶつかん）は、[[宮崎県]][[西都市]][[大字]]三宅[[小字|字]]西都原西に所在する[[考古学]]系[[博物館]]。国の[[特別史跡]]・[[西都原古墳群]]そばにあり、同古墳群についての展示を主とし、宮崎県内の[[遺跡]]や[[考古学]]についての催しを行う。
== 概要 == 宮崎県の中央部、西都原台地に広がる西都原古墳群は、[[陵墓参考地]]の[[男狭穂塚古墳]]・[[女狭穂塚古墳]]を含めて319基が造営された巨大[[古墳群]]である<ref>{{Cite web|title=宮崎県立西都原考古博物館｜西都原古墳群の概要|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/guidance.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>。西都原考古博物館は、当古墳群の[[考古資料]]を収蔵・保管・展示するほか、[[古墳時代]]や他の時代の様々な考古学系テーマを取り上げ公開していくことをコンセプトとして、2004年（平成16年）4月17日に開館した<ref>{{Cite web|title=宮崎県立西都原考古博物館｜企画展アーカイブ|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/exhibition.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>。
=== 特徴 === [[日本列島]]全般の古墳時代についての展示のほか、 [[南九州]]に特徴的な[[地下式横穴墓]]を含めた西都原古墳群についての展示、[[律令制|律令期]]（[[飛鳥時代|飛鳥]]・[[奈良時代]]）に[[化外の地|化外の民]]として扱われた[[隼人]]についての展示も行っている。年に数回の企画展示のほか、考古学系講座や講演会・体験イベントを実施する。
「常設展示」という概念を廃し、常に新しい情報を展示・提供する、として「常新展示」という概念を導入している{{Sfn|北郷|2004|pp=1-2}}。また遺跡から出土した資料に直接触れて鑑賞し、体感的に学ぶことを目的とした「ハンズオン展示」や、館内展示・設備の位置などを造形的に示した立体サイン（触察ピクト）を設置し、視覚・聴覚障害者にも利用しやすく、また来館者自らが主体的に学べることを目的とした[[ユニバーサルデザイン]]を導入している<ref>{{Cite web|title=宮崎県立西都原考古博物館｜ユニバーサルデザイン|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/universal.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>。そのため、展示室には基本的に順路や導線が存在しない。
== 付属施設 == [[ファイル:Saitobaru-kofungun_yokoana-bogun.JPG|サムネイル|280x280ピクセル|酒元ノ上[[横穴墓]]群遺構保存覆屋（2013年8月24日）]] [[ファイル:Saitobaru_Konohanakan.jpg|サムネイル|280x280ピクセル|このはな館（2019年1月4日）]]
* 古代生活体験館 * 西都原ガイダンスセンター「このはな館」 * 酒元ノ上[[横穴墓]]群遺構保存覆屋 * 西都原111号墳・4号[[地下式横穴墓]]保存見学施設
== 利用案内 == * 入場料：無料 * 開館時間：午前10時から午後6時まで * 休館日：月曜日・祝日の翌日・年末年始（12月28日から翌1月4日まで） * 駐車場：普通車100台、大型バス8台、車椅子専用駐車場8台<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=宮崎県立西都原考古博物館｜利用案内|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/usage.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>
== アクセス == * バス：[[宮交シティ]]より「西都バスセンター（西都原考古博物館前経由）」行き乗車（約70分）。「西都原考古博物館前」下車 * 自動車：[[宮崎市]]から[[国道219号線]]約40分。[[東九州自動車道]][[西都インターチェンジ]]約10分<ref name=":0" />
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite journal|和書|last=北郷|first=泰道|year=2004|date=2004-06-30|title=常設展示をなくそう|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/collumn/007.pdf|journal=西都原考古博物館コラム|volume=7|pages=1-2|publisher=西都原考古博物館|format=PDF|ref=harv}}
== 関連項目 == * [[西都原古墳群]]
== 外部リンク == * [https://www.saito-kanko.jp/sightseeing/%e5%ae%ae%e5%b4%8e%e7%9c%8c%e7%ab%8b%e8%a5%bf%e9%83%bd%e5%8e%9f%e8%80%83%e5%8f%a4%e5%8d%9a%e7%89%a9%e9%a4%a8 一般社団法人・西都市観光協会] * [http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/index.html 公式サイト]

revision.text:
{{博物館 |名称=宮崎県立西都原考古博物館 |native_name=The Saitobaru Archeological Museum of Miyazaki Prefecture |native_name_lang=en |imagesize=280px |画像=File:Saitobaru Archaeology Museum.jpg |画像説明=正面入り口 {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord2|32|07|31.3|N|131|22|56.6|E}}}} |正式名称=宮崎県立西都原考古博物館 |愛称= |前身=西都原資料館 |専門分野=歴史学・考古学・西都原古墳群 |収蔵作品数= |来館者数= |館長= |学芸員= |研究職員= |事業主体=[[宮崎県]] |管理運営= |年運営費= |建物設計=鉄筋コンクリート造4階建て（地下1階、地上3階） |延床面積=6,678.63m<sup>2</sup> |開館=2004年（平成16年）4月17日 |閉館= |所在地郵便番号=881-0005 |所在地=宮崎県西都市大字三宅字西都原西5670番 |緯度度=32|緯度分=07|緯度秒=31.3 |経度度=131|経度分=22|経度秒=56.6 |アクセス= |URL=[http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/index.html 公式サイト] }} '''宮崎県立西都原考古博物館'''（みやざきけんりつさいとばるこうこはくぶつかん）は、[[宮崎県]][[西都市]][[大字]]三宅[[小字|字]]西都原西に所在する[[考古学]]系[[博物館]]。国の[[特別史跡]]・[[西都原古墳群]]そばにあり、同古墳群についての展示を主とし、宮崎県内の[[遺跡]]や[[考古学]]についての催しを行う。
== 概要 == 宮崎県の中央部、西都原台地に広がる西都原古墳群は、[[陵墓参考地]]の[[男狭穂塚古墳]]・[[女狭穂塚古墳]]を含めて319基が造営された巨大[[古墳群]]である<ref>{{Cite web|title=宮崎県立西都原考古博物館｜西都原古墳群の概要|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/guidance.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>。西都原考古博物館は、当古墳群の[[考古資料]]を収蔵・保管・展示するほか、[[古墳時代]]や他の時代の様々な考古学系テーマを取り上げ公開していくことをコンセプトとして、2004年（平成16年）4月17日に開館した<ref>{{Cite web|title=宮崎県立西都原考古博物館｜企画展アーカイブ|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/exhibition.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>。
=== 特徴 === [[日本列島]]全般の古墳時代についての展示のほか、 [[南九州]]に特徴的な[[地下式横穴墓]]を含めた西都原古墳群についての展示、[[律令制|律令期]]（[[飛鳥時代|飛鳥]]・[[奈良時代]]）に[[化外の地|化外の民]]として扱われた[[隼人]]についての展示も行っている。年に数回の企画展示のほか、考古学系講座や講演会・体験イベントを実施する。
「常設展示」という概念を廃し、常に新しい情報を展示・提供する、として「常新展示」という概念を導入している{{Sfn|北郷|2004|pp=1-2}}。また遺跡から出土した資料に直接触れて鑑賞し、体感的に学ぶことを目的とした「ハンズオン展示」や、館内展示・設備の位置などを造形的に示した立体サイン（触察ピクト）を設置し、視覚・聴覚障害者にも利用しやすく、また来館者自らが主体的に学べることを目的とした[[ユニバーサルデザイン]]を導入している<ref>{{Cite web|title=宮崎県立西都原考古博物館｜ユニバーサルデザイン|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/universal.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>。そのため、展示室には基本的に順路や動線が存在しない。
== 付属施設 == [[ファイル:Saitobaru-kofungun_yokoana-bogun.JPG|サムネイル|280x280ピクセル|酒元ノ上[[横穴墓]]群遺構保存覆屋（2013年8月24日）]] [[ファイル:Saitobaru_Konohanakan.jpg|サムネイル|280x280ピクセル|このはな館（2019年1月4日）]]
* 古代生活体験館 * 西都原ガイダンスセンター「このはな館」 * 酒元ノ上[[横穴墓]]群遺構保存覆屋 * 西都原111号墳・4号[[地下式横穴墓]]保存見学施設
== 利用案内 == * 入場料：無料 * 開館時間：午前10時から午後6時まで * 休館日：月曜日・祝日の翌日・年末年始（12月28日から翌1月4日まで） * 駐車場：普通車100台、大型バス8台、車椅子専用駐車場8台<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=宮崎県立西都原考古博物館｜利用案内|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/usage.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>
== アクセス == * バス：[[宮交シティ]]より「西都バスセンター（西都原考古博物館前経由）」行き乗車（約70分）。「西都原考古博物館前」下車 * 自動車：[[宮崎市]]から[[国道219号線]]約40分。[[東九州自動車道]][[西都インターチェンジ]]約10分<ref name=":0" />
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite journal|和書|last=北郷|first=泰道|year=2004|date=2004-06-30|title=常設展示をなくそう|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/collumn/007.pdf|journal=西都原考古博物館コラム|volume=7|pages=1-2|publisher=西都原考古博物館|format=PDF|ref=harv}}
== 関連項目 == * [[西都原古墳群]]
== 外部リンク == * [https://www.saito-kanko.jp/sightseeing/%e5%ae%ae%e5%b4%8e%e7%9c%8c%e7%ab%8b%e8%a5%bf%e9%83%bd%e5%8e%9f%e8%80%83%e5%8f%a4%e5%8d%9a%e7%89%a9%e9%a4%a8 一般社団法人・西都市観光協会] * [http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/index.html 公式サイト]
{{考古学}} {{博物館法の博物館}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みやさきけんりつさいとはるこうこはくふつかん}} [[Category:日本の考古博物館]] [[Category:日本の公立博物館]] [[Category:宮崎県の博物館]] [[Category:2004年開設の博物館]] [[Category:西都市の教育]] [[Category:西都市の建築物]] [[Category:西都市の歴史]]

revision.comment: Botによる: [[Special:Permalink/83490336#Maplink2とOSM Location mapテンプレートに渡す引数におけるCoord2テンプレートをCoordテンプレートに置換する|Bot作業依頼]] {{[[Template:Maplink2|Maplink2]]}}と{{[[Template:OSM Location map|OSM Location map]]}}に渡す座標を{{[[Template:Coord2|Coord2]]}}から{{[[Template:Coord|Coord]]}}に変更

== 利用案内 == * 入場料：無料 * 開館時間：午前10時から午後6時まで * 休館日：月曜日・祝日の翌日・年末年始（12月28日から翌1月4日まで） * 駐車場：普通車100台、大型バス8台、車椅子専用駐車場8台<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=宮崎県立西都原考古博物館｜利用案内|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/usage.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>
== アクセス == * バス：[[宮交シティ]]より「西都バスセンター（西都原考古博物館前経由）」行き乗車（約70分）。「西都原考古博物館前」下車 * 自動車：[[宮崎市]]から[[国道219号線]]約40分。[[東九州自動車道]][[西都インターチェンジ]]約10分<ref name=":0" />
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite journal|和書|last=北郷|first=泰道|year=2004|date=2004-06-30|title=常設展示をなくそう|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/collumn/007.pdf|journal=西都原考古博物館コラム|volume=7|pages=1-2|publisher=西都原考古博物館|format=PDF|ref=harv}}
== 関連項目 == * [[西都原古墳群]]
== 外部リンク == * [https://www.saito-kanko.jp/sightseeing/%e5%ae%ae%e5%b4%8e%e7%9c%8c%e7%ab%8b%e8%a5%bf%e9%83%bd%e5%8e%9f%e8%80%83%e5%8f%a4%e5%8d%9a%e7%89%a9%e9%a4%a8 一般社団法人・西都市観光協会] * [http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/index.html 公式サイト]
{{考古学}} {{博物館法の博物館}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みやさきけんりつさいとはるこうこはくふつかん}} [[Category:日本の考古博物館]] [[Category:日本の公立博物館]] [[Category:宮崎県の博物館]] [[Category:2004年開設の博物館]] [[Category:西都市の教育]] [[Category:西都市の建築物]] [[Category:西都市の歴史]]

{{博物館 |名称=宮崎県立西都原考古博物館 |native_name=The Saitobaru Archeological Museum of Miyazaki Prefecture |native_name_lang=en |imagesize=280px |画像=File:Saitobaru Archaeology Museum.jpg |画像説明=正面入り口 |正式名称=宮崎県立西都原考古博物館 |愛称= |前身=西都原資料館 |専門分野=歴史学・考古学・西都原古墳群 |収蔵作品数= |来館者数= |館長= |学芸員= |研究職員= |事業主体=[[宮崎県]] |管理運営= |年運営費= |建物設計=鉄筋コンクリート造4階建て（地下1階、地上3階） |延床面積=6,678.63m<sup>2</sup> |開館=2004年（平成16年）4月17日 |閉館= |所在地郵便番号=881-0005 |所在地=宮崎県西都市大字三宅字西都原西5670番 |緯度度=32|緯度分=07|緯度秒=31.3 |経度度=131|経度分=22|経度秒=56.6 |アクセス= |URL=[http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/index.html 公式サイト] }} '''宮崎県立西都原考古博物館'''（みやざきけんりつさいとばるこうこはくぶつかん）は、[[宮崎県]][[西都市]][[大字]]三宅[[小字|字]]西都原西に所在する公立[[考古学]]系[[博物館]]。国の[[特別史跡]]・[[西都原古墳群]]そばにあり、同古墳群についての展示を主とし、宮崎県内の[[遺跡]]や[[考古学]]についての催しを行う。
== 概要 == 宮崎県の中央部、西都原台地に広がる西都原古墳群は、[[陵墓参考地]]の[[男狭穂塚古墳]]・[[女狭穂塚古墳]]を含めて319基が造営された巨大[[古墳群]]である<ref>{{Cite web|title=宮崎県立西都原考古博物館｜西都原古墳群の概要|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/guidance.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>。西都原考古博物館は、当古墳群の[[考古資料]]を収蔵・保管・展示するほか、[[古墳時代]]や他の時代の様々な考古学系テーマを取り上げ公開していくことをコンセプトとして、2004年（平成16年）4月17日に開館した<ref>{{Cite web|title=宮崎県立西都原考古博物館｜企画展アーカイブ|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/exhibition.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>。
=== 特徴 === [[日本列島]]全般の古墳時代についての展示のほか、 [[南九州]]に特徴的な[[地下式横穴墓]]を含めた西都原古墳群についての展示、[[律令制|律令期]]（[[飛鳥時代|飛鳥]]・[[奈良時代]]）に[[化外の地|化外の民]]として扱われた[[隼人]]についての展示も行っている。年に数回の企画展示のほか、考古学系講座や講演会・体験イベントを実施する。
「常設展示」という概念を廃し、常に新しい情報を展示・提供する、として「常新展示」という概念を導入している{{Sfn|北郷|2004|pp=1-2}}。また遺跡から出土した資料に直接触れて鑑賞し、体感的に学ぶことを目的とした「ハンズオン展示」や、館内展示・設備の位置などを造形的に示した立体サイン（触察ピクト）を設置し、視覚・聴覚障害者にも利用しやすく、また来館者自らが主体的に学べることを目的とした[[ユニバーサルデザイン]]を導入している<ref>{{Cite web|title=宮崎県立西都原考古博物館｜ユニバーサルデザイン|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/universal.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>。そのため、展示室には基本的に順路や動線が存在しない。
== 付属施設 == [[ファイル:Saitobaru-kofungun_yokoana-bogun.JPG|サムネイル|280x280ピクセル|酒元ノ上[[横穴墓]]群遺構保存覆屋（2013年8月24日）]] [[ファイル:Saitobaru_Konohanakan.jpg|サムネイル|280x280ピクセル|このはな館（2019年1月4日）]]
* 古代生活体験館 * 西都原ガイダンスセンター「このはな館」 * 酒元ノ上[[横穴墓]]群遺構保存覆屋 * 西都原111号墳・4号[[地下式横穴墓]]保存見学施設
== 利用案内 == * 入場料：無料 * 開館時間：午前10時から午後6時まで * 休館日：月曜日・祝日の翌日・年末年始（12月28日から翌1月4日まで） * 駐車場：普通車100台、大型バス8台、車椅子専用駐車場8台<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=宮崎県立西都原考古博物館｜利用案内|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/usage.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>
== アクセス == * バス：[[宮交シティ]]より「西都バスセンター（西都原考古博物館前経由）」行き乗車（約70分）。「西都原考古博物館前」下車 * 自動車：[[宮崎市]]から[[国道219号線]]約40分。[[東九州自動車道]][[西都インターチェンジ]]約10分<ref name=":0" />
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite journal|和書|last=北郷|first=泰道|year=2004|date=2004-06-30|title=常設展示をなくそう|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/collumn/007.pdf|journal=西都原考古博物館コラム|volume=7|pages=1-2|publisher=西都原考古博物館|format=PDF|ref=harv}}

{{博物館 |名称=宮崎県立西都原考古博物館 |native_name=The Saitobaru Archeological Museum of Miyazaki Prefecture |native_name_lang=en |imagesize=280px |画像=File:Saitobaru Archaeology Museum.jpg |画像説明=正面入り口 |正式名称=宮崎県立西都原考古博物館 |愛称= |前身=西都原資料館 |専門分野=歴史学・考古学・西都原古墳群 |収蔵作品数= |来館者数= |館長= |学芸員= |研究職員= |事業主体=[[宮崎県]] |管理運営= |年運営費= |建物設計=鉄筋コンクリート造4階建て（地下1階、地上3階） |延床面積=6,678.63m<sup>2</sup> |開館=2004年（平成16年）4月17日 |閉館= |所在地郵便番号=881-0005 |所在地=宮崎県西都市大字三宅字西都原西5670番 |緯度度=32|緯度分=07|緯度秒=31.3 |経度度=131|経度分=22|経度秒=56.6 |アクセス= |URL=[http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/index.html 公式サイト] }} '''宮崎県立西都原考古博物館'''（みやざきけんりつさいとばるこうこはくぶつかん）は、[[宮崎県]][[西都市]][[大字]]三宅[[小字|字]]西都原西に所在する公立[[考古学]]系[[博物館]]。国の[[特別史跡]]・[[西都原古墳群]]そばにあり、同古墳群についての展示を主とし、宮崎県内の[[遺跡]]や[[考古学]]についての催しを行う。
== 概要 == 宮崎県の中央部、西都原台地に広がる西都原古墳群は、[[陵墓参考地]]の[[男狭穂塚古墳]]・[[女狭穂塚古墳]]を含めて319基が造営された巨大[[古墳群]]である<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=宮崎県立西都原考古博物館｜西都原古墳群の概要|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/guidance.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>。西都原考古博物館は、当古墳群の[[考古資料]]を収蔵・保管・展示するほか、[[古墳時代]]や他の時代の様々な考古学系テーマを取り上げ公開していくことをコンセプトとして、2004年（平成16年）4月17日に開館した<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=宮崎県立西都原考古博物館｜企画展アーカイブ|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/exhibition.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>。
=== 特徴 === [[日本列島]]全般の古墳時代についての展示のほか、 [[南九州]]に特徴的な[[地下式横穴墓]]を含めた西都原古墳群についての展示、[[律令制|律令期]]（[[飛鳥時代|飛鳥]]・[[奈良時代]]）に[[化外の地|化外の民]]として扱われた[[隼人]]についての展示も行っている。年に数回の企画展示のほか、考古学系講座や講演会・体験イベントを実施する。
「常設展示」という概念を廃し、常に新しい情報を展示・提供する、として「常新展示」という概念を導入している{{Sfn|北郷|2004|pp=1-2}}。また遺跡から出土した資料に直接触れて鑑賞し、体感的に学ぶことを目的とした「ハンズオン展示」や、館内展示・設備の位置などを造形的に示した立体サイン（触察ピクト）を設置し、視覚・聴覚障害者にも利用しやすく、また来館者自らが主体的に学べることを目的とした[[ユニバーサルデザイン]]を導入している<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=宮崎県立西都原考古博物館｜ユニバーサルデザイン|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/universal.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>。そのため、展示室には基本的に順路や動線が存在しない。
== 付属施設 == [[ファイル:Saitobaru-kofungun_yokoana-bogun.JPG|サムネイル|280x280ピクセル|酒元ノ上[[横穴墓]]群遺構保存覆屋（2013年8月24日）]] [[ファイル:Saitobaru_Konohanakan.jpg|サムネイル|280x280ピクセル|このはな館（2019年1月4日）]]
* 古代生活体験館 * 西都原ガイダンスセンター「このはな館」 * 酒元ノ上[[横穴墓]]群遺構保存覆屋 * 西都原111号墳・4号[[地下式横穴墓]]保存見学施設
== 利用案内 == * 入場料：無料 * 開館時間：午前10時から午後6時まで * 休館日：月曜日・祝日の翌日・年末年始（12月28日から翌1月4日まで） * 駐車場：普通車100台、大型バス8台、車椅子専用駐車場8台<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=宮崎県立西都原考古博物館｜利用案内|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/usage.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>
== アクセス == * バス：[[宮交シティ]]より「西都バスセンター（西都原考古博物館前経由）」行き乗車（約70分）。「西都原考古博物館前」下車 * 自動車：[[宮崎市]]から[[国道219号線]]約40分。[[東九州自動車道]][[西都インターチェンジ]]約10分<ref name=":0" />
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite journal|和書|last=北郷|first=泰道|year=2004|date=2004-06-30|title=常設展示をなくそう|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/collumn/007.pdf|journal=西都原考古博物館コラム|volume=7|pages=1-2|publisher=西都原考古博物館|format=PDF|ref=harv}}
== 関連項目 == * [[西都原古墳群]]
== 外部リンク == * [https://www.saito-kanko.jp/sightseeing/%e5%ae%ae%e5%b4%8e%e7%9c%8c%e7%ab%8b%e8%a5%bf%e9%83%bd%e5%8e%9f%e8%80%83%e5%8f%a4%e5%8d%9a%e7%89%a9%e9%a4%a8 一般社団法人・西都市観光協会] * [http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/index.html 公式サイト]
{{考古学}} {{博物館法の博物館}} {{Normdaten}}

revision.text:
{{Museum | Name = Miyazaki Prefectural Saitobaru Archaeological Museum | native_name = The Saitobaru Archaeological Museum of Miyazaki Prefecture | native_name_lang = en | imagesize = 280px | image = File:Saitobaru Archaeology Museum.jpg | image_caption = Front entrance | official_name = 宮崎県立西都原考古博物館 | alias = | predecessor = Saitobaru Resource Center | field = History, Archaeology, Saitobaru Kofun Group | collection_size = | visitors = | director = | curators = | research_staff = | operated_by = [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] | management = | annual_budget = | building_design = Reinforced concrete, 4 stories (1 basement, 3 above ground) | total_floor_area = 6,678.63 m² | opening_date = April 17, 2004 (Heisei 16) | closing_date = | postal_code = 881-0005 | address = Miyake Aza Saitobaru Nishi 5670, Saito City, Miyazaki Prefecture | latitude_deg = 32 | latitude_min = 07 | latitude_sec = 31.3 | longitude_deg = 131 | longitude_min = 22 | longitude_sec = 56.6 | access = | website = [http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/index.html Official website] }}
'''Miyazaki Prefectural Saitobaru Archaeological Museum''' (宮崎県立西都原考古博物館, ''Miyazaki Kenritsu Saitobaru Kōko Hakubutsukan'') is a public [[archaeological museum]] located in Miyake Aza Saitobaru Nishi, [[Saito City]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]], Japan. Situated near the [[Special Historic Site]]-designated [[Saitobaru Kofun Group]], the museum primarily exhibits materials related to the kofun group while hosting events on [[archaeology]] and [[historical sites]] throughout Miyazaki Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The Saitobaru Kofun Group, located in central Miyazaki on the Saitobaru Plateau, is a massive collection of 319 [[kofun]] (burial mounds), including the imperial tomb references [[Osahozuka Kofun]] and [[Mesahozuka Kofun]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=Overview of the Saitobaru Kofun Group|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/guidance.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>. Opened on April 17, 2004, the Saitobaru Archaeological Museum was established to house, preserve, and display archaeological materials from the kofun group. The museum also explores various archaeological themes from the [[Kofun period]] and beyond<ref>{{Cite web|title=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Exhibition Archives|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/exhibition.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>.
=== Features ===
In addition to exhibits covering the Kofun period across the [[Japanese archipelago]], the museum showcases unique burial practices from [[Southern Kyushu]], such as [[underground horizontal chamber tombs]] specific to the Saitobaru Kofun Group. Other exhibits delve into the [[Hayato people]] of the [[Asuka period|Asuka]] and [[Nara period]]s, who were considered [[barbarians]] under the [[Ritsuryō system]].
The museum conducts periodic special exhibitions, archaeology lectures, workshops, and experiential learning events. A unique "ever-changing exhibition" concept replaces the traditional idea of "permanent exhibitions," ensuring new information is continuously displayed<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Kitagō|first=Yasumichi|year=2004|title=Abolishing Permanent Exhibitions|journal=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Column|volume=7|pages=1-2|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/collumn/007.pdf|format=PDF|ref=harv}}</ref>.
Universal design principles enhance accessibility, including tactile signs and displays for visitors with visual or hearing impairments. The "hands-on exhibits" allow direct interaction with artifacts to promote experiential learning<ref>{{Cite web|title=Universal Design at the Saitobaru Archaeological Museum|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/universal.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>. The exhibit areas are intentionally designed without fixed routes or pathways.
== Facilities == [[File:Saitobaru-kofungun_yokoana-bogun.JPG|thumb|280px|Sakemotonoue Underground Chamber Tomb Group Preservation Building (2013)]] [[File:Saitobaru_Konohanakan.jpg|thumb|280px|Konohana Hall (2019)]] * Ancient Living Experience Center * Saitobaru Guidance Center "Konohana Hall" * Sakemotonoue Underground Chamber Tomb Preservation Building * Saitobaru No.111 Tomb and No.4 [[Underground Horizontal Chamber Tomb]] Preservation Facility
== Visitor Information == * Admission: Free * Opening Hours: 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM * Closed: Mondays, the day following public holidays, and year-end/New Year holidays (December 28 to January 4) * Parking: 100 spaces for regular vehicles, 8 for large buses, and 8 wheelchair-accessible spaces<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Visitor Information|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/usage.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>
== Access == * **Bus**: From [[Miyako City]], board the "Saito Bus Center (via Saitobaru Archaeological Museum)" route (approximately 70 minutes). Alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." * **Car**: Approximately 40 minutes from [[Miyazaki City]] via [[National Route 219]] or 10 minutes from the [[Nishitō Interchange]] on the [[Higashikyushu Expressway]]<ref name=":0" />.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Citation help}}

== External Links == * [https://www.saito-kanko.jp/sightseeing/%e5%ae%ae%e5%b4%8e%e7%9c%8c%e7%ab%8b%e8%a5%bf%e9%83%bd%e5%8e%9f%e8%80%83%e5%8f%a4%e5%8d%9a%e7%89%a9%e9%a4%a8 Saito City Tourism Association] * [http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/index.html Official website]

== Visitor Information == * Admission: Free * Opening Hours: 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM * Closed: Mondays, the day following public holidays, and year-end/New Year holidays (December 28 to [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[category:Unsorted link categories]] January 4) * Parking: 100 spaces for regular vehicles, 8 for large buses, and 8 wheelchair-accessible spaces<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Visitor Information|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/usage.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>
== Access == * **Bus**: From [[Miyako City]], board the "Saito Bus Center (via Saitobaru Archaeological Museum)" route (approximately 70 minutes). Alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." * **Car**: Approximately 40 minutes from [[Miyazaki City]] via [[National Route 219]] or 10 minutes from the [[Nishitō Interchange]] on the [[Higashikyushu Expressway]]<ref name=":0" />.

revision.text:
{{Museum | Name = Miyazaki Prefectural Saitobaru Archaeological Museum | native_name = The Saitobaru Archaeological Museum of Miyazaki Prefecture | native_name_lang = en | imagesize = 280px | image = File:Saitobaru Archaeology Museum.jpg | image_caption = Front entrance | official_name = 宮崎県立西都原考古博物館 | alias = | predecessor = Saitobaru Resource Center | field = History, Archaeology, Saitobaru Kofun Group | collection_size = | visitors = | director = | curators = | research_staff = | operated_by = [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] | management = | annual_budget = | building_design = Reinforced concrete, 4 stories (1 basement, 3 above ground) | total_floor_area = 6,678.63 m² | opening_date = April 17, 2004 (Heisei 16) | closing_date = | postal_code = 881-0005 | address = Miyake Aza Saitobaru Nishi 5670, Saito City, Miyazaki Prefecture | latitude_deg = 32 | latitude_min = 07 | latitude_sec = 31.3 | longitude_deg = 131 | longitude_min = 22 | longitude_sec = 56.6 | access = | website = [http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/index.html Official website] }}
'''Miyazaki Prefectural Saitobaru Archaeological Museum''' (宮崎県立西都原考古博物館, ''Miyazaki Kenritsu Saitobaru Kōko Hakubutsukan'') is a public [[archaeological museum]] located in Miyake Aza Saitobaru Nishi, [[Saito City]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]], Japan. Situated near the [[Special Historic Site]]-designated [[Saitobaru Kofun Group]], the museum primarily exhibits materials related to the kofun group while hosting events on [[archaeology]] and [[historical sites]] throughout Miyazaki Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The Saitobaru Kofun Group, located in central Miyazaki on the Saitobaru Plateau, is a massive collection of 319 [[kofun]] (burial mounds), including the imperial tomb references [[Osahozuka Kofun]] and [[Mesahozuka Kofun]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=Overview of the Saitobaru Kofun Group|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/guidance.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>. Opened on April 17, 2004, the Saitobaru Archaeological Museum was established to house, preserve, and display archaeological materials from the kofun group. The museum also explores various archaeological themes from the [[Kofun period]] and beyond<ref>{{Cite web|title=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Exhibition Archives|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/exhibition.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>.
=== Features ===
In addition to exhibits covering the Kofun period across the [[Japanese archipelago]], the museum showcases unique burial practices from [[Southern Kyushu]], such as [[underground horizontal chamber tombs]] specific to the Saitobaru Kofun Group. Other exhibits delve into the [[Hayato people]] of the [[Asuka period|Asuka]] and [[Nara period]]s, who were considered [[barbarians]] under the [[Ritsuryō system]].
The museum conducts periodic special exhibitions, archaeology lectures, workshops, and experiential learning events. A unique "ever-changing exhibition" concept replaces the traditional idea of "permanent exhibitions," ensuring new information is continuously displayed<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Kitagō|first=Yasumichi|year=2004|title=Abolishing Permanent Exhibitions|journal=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Column|volume=7|pages=1-2|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/collumn/007.pdf|format=PDF|ref=harv}}</ref>.
Universal design principles enhance accessibility, including tactile signs and displays for visitors with visual or hearing impairments. The "hands-on exhibits" allow direct interaction with artifacts to promote experiential learning<ref>{{Cite web|title=Universal Design at the Saitobaru Archaeological Museum|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/universal.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>. The exhibit areas are intentionally designed without fixed routes or pathways.
== Facilities == [[File:Saitobaru-kofungun_yokoana-bogun.JPG|thumb|280px|Sakemotonoue Underground Chamber Tomb Group Preservation Building (2013)]] [[File:Saitobaru_Konohanakan.jpg|thumb|280px|Konohana Hall (2019)]] * Ancient Living Experience Center * Saitobaru Guidance Center "Konohana Hall" * Sakemotonoue Underground Chamber Tomb Preservation Building * Saitobaru No.111 Tomb and No.4 [[Underground Horizontal Chamber Tomb]] Preservation Facility
== Visitor Information == * Admission: Free * Opening Hours: 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM * Closed: Mondays, the day following public holidays, and year-end/New Year holidays (December 28 to [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[category:Unsorted link categories]] January 4) * Parking: 100 spaces for regular vehicles, 8 for large buses, and 8 wheelchair-accessible spaces<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Visitor Information|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/usage.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>
== Access == * **Bus**: From [[Miyako City]], board the "Saito Bus Center (via Saitobaru Archaeological Museum)" route (approximately 70 minutes). Alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." * **Car**: Approximately 40 minutes from [[Miyazaki City]] via [[National Route 219]] or 10 minutes from the [[Nishitō Interchange]] on the [[Higashikyushu Expressway]]<ref name=":0" />.

revision.text:
{{Museum | Name = Miyazaki Prefectural Saitobaru Archaeological Museum | native_name = The Saitobaru Archaeological Museum of Miyazaki Prefecture | native_name_lang = en | imagesize = 280px | image = File:Saitobaru Archaeology Museum.jpg | image_caption = Front entrance | official_name = 宮崎県立西都原考古博物館 | alias = | predecessor = Saitobaru Resource Center | field = History, Archaeology, Saitobaru Kofun Group | collection_size = | visitors = | director = | curators = | research_staff = | operated_by = [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] | management = | annual_budget = | building_design = Reinforced concrete, 4 stories (1 basement, 3 above ground) | total_floor_area = 6,678.63 m² | opening_date = April 17, 2004 (Heisei 16) | closing_date = | postal_code = 881-0005 | address = Miyake Aza Saitobaru Nishi 5670, Saito City, Miyazaki Prefecture | latitude_deg = 32 | latitude_min = 07 | latitude_sec = 31.3 | longitude_deg = 131 | longitude_min = 22 | longitude_sec = 56.6 | access = | website = [http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/index.html Official website] }}
'''Miyazaki Prefectural Saitobaru Archaeological Museum''' (宮崎県立西都原考古博物館, ''Miyazaki Kenritsu Saitobaru Kōko Hakubutsukan'') is a public [[archaeological museum]] located in Miyake Aza Saitobaru Nishi, [[Saito City]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]], Japan. Situated near the [[Special Historic Site]]-designated [[Saitobaru Kofun Group]], the museum primarily exhibits materials related to the kofun group while hosting events on [[archaeology]] and [[historical sites]] throughout Miyazaki Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The Saitobaru Kofun Group, located in central Miyazaki on the Saitobaru Plateau, is a massive collection of 319 [[kofun]] (burial mounds), including the imperial tomb references [[Osahozuka Kofun]] and [[Mesahozuka Kofun]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=Overview of the Saitobaru Kofun Group|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/guidance.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>. Opened on April 17, 2004, the Saitobaru Archaeological Museum was established to house, preserve, and display archaeological materials from the kofun group. The museum also explores various archaeological themes from the [[Kofun period]] and beyond<ref>{{Cite web|title=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Exhibition Archives|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/exhibition.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>.
=== Features ===
In addition to exhibits covering the Kofun period across the [[Japanese archipelago]], the museum showcases unique burial practices from [[Southern Kyushu]], such as [[underground horizontal chamber tombs]] specific to the Saitobaru Kofun Group. Other exhibits delve into the [[Hayato people]] of the [[Asuka period|Asuka]] and [[Nara period]]s, who were considered [[barbarians]] under the [[Ritsuryō system]].
The museum conducts periodic special exhibitions, archaeology lectures, workshops, and experiential learning events. A unique "ever-changing exhibition" concept replaces the traditional idea of "permanent exhibitions," ensuring new information is continuously displayed<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Kitagō|first=Yasumichi|year=2004|title=Abolishing Permanent Exhibitions|journal=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Column|volume=7|pages=1-2|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/collumn/007.pdf|format=PDF|ref=harv}}</ref>.
Universal design principles enhance accessibility, including tactile signs and displays for visitors with visual or hearing impairments. The "hands-on exhibits" allow direct interaction with artifacts to promote experiential learning<ref>{{Cite web|title=Universal Design at the Saitobaru Archaeological Museum|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/universal.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>. The exhibit areas are intentionally designed without fixed routes or pathways.
== Facilities == [[File:Saitobaru-kofungun_yokoana-bogun.JPG|thumb|280px|Sakemotonoue Underground Chamber Tomb Group Preservation Building (2013)]] [[File:Saitobaru_Konohanakan.jpg|thumb|280px|Konohana Hall (2019)]] * Ancient Living Experience Center * Saitobaru Guidance Center "Konohana Hall" * Sakemotonoue Underground Chamber Tomb Preservation Building * Saitobaru No.111 Tomb and No.4 [[Underground Horizontal Chamber Tomb]] Preservation Facility
== Visitor Information == * Admission: Free * Opening Hours: 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM * Closed: Mondays, the day following public holidays, and year-end/New Year holidays (December 28 to [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] January 4) * Parking: 100 spaces for regular vehicles, 8 for large buses, and 8 wheelchair-accessible spaces<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Visitor Information|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/usage.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>
== Access == * **Bus**: From [[Miyako City]], board the "Saito Bus Center (via Saitobaru Archaeological Museum)" route (approximately 70 minutes). Alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." * **Car**: Approximately 40 minutes from [[Miyazaki City]] via [[National Route 219]] or 10 minutes from the [[Nishitō Interchange]] on the [[Higashikyushu Expressway]]<ref name=":0" />.

A unique "ever-changing exhibition" concept replaces the traditional idea of "permanent exhibitions," ensuring new information is continuously displayed<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Kitagō|first=Yasumichi|year=2004|title=Abolishing Permanent Exhibitions|journal=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Column|volume=7|pages=1-2|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/collumn/007.pdf|format=PDF|ref=harv}}</ref>.
Universal design principles enhance accessibility, including tactile signs and displays for visitors with visual or hearing impairments. The "hands-on exhibits" allow direct interaction with artifacts to promote experiential learning<ref>{{Cite web|title=Universal Design at the Saitobaru Archaeological Museum|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/universal.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>. The exhibit areas are intentionally designed without fixed routes or pathways.

{{Museum | Name = Miyazaki Prefectural Saitobaru Archaeological Museum | native_name = The Saitobaru Archaeological Museum of Miyazaki Prefecture | native_name_lang = en | imagesize = 280px | image = File:Saitobaru Archaeology Museum.jpg | image_caption = Front entrance | official_name = 宮崎県立西都原考古博物館 | alias = | predecessor = Saitobaru Resource Center | field = History, Archaeology, Saitobaru Kofun Group | collection_size = | visitors = | director = | curators = | research_staff = | operated_by = [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] | management = | annual_budget = | building_design = Reinforced concrete, 4 stories (1 basement, 3 above ground) | total_floor_area = 6,678.63 m² | opening_date = April 17, 2004 (Heisei 16) | closing_date = | postal_code = 881-0005 | address = Miyake Aza Saitobaru Nishi 5670, Saito City, Miyazaki Prefecture | latitude_deg = 32 | latitude_min = 07 | latitude_sec = 31.3 | longitude_deg = 131 | longitude_min = 22 | longitude_sec = 56.6 | access = | website = [http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/index.html Official website] }}
'''Miyazaki Prefectural Saitobaru Archaeological Museum''' (宮崎県立西都原考古博物館, ''Miyazaki Kenritsu Saitobaru Kōko Hakubutsukan'') is a public [[archaeological museum]] located in Miyake Aza Saitobaru Nishi, [[Saito City]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]], Japan. Situated near the [[Special Historic Site]]-designated [[Saitobaru Kofun Group]], the museum primarily exhibits materials related to the kofun group while hosting events on [[archaeology]] and [[historical sites]] throughout Miyazaki Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The Saitobaru Kofun Group, located in central Miyazaki on the Saitobaru Plateau, is a massive collection of 319 [[kofun]] (burial mounds), including the imperial tomb references [[Osahozuka Kofun]] and [[Mesahozuka Kofun]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=Overview of the Saitobaru Kofun Group|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/guidance.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>. Opened on April 17, 2004, the Saitobaru Archaeological Museum was established to house, preserve, and display archaeological materials from the kofun group. The museum also explores various archaeological themes from the [[Kofun period]] and beyond<ref>{{Cite web|title=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Exhibition Archives|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/exhibition.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>.
=== Features ===
In addition to exhibits covering the Kofun period across the [[Japanese archipelago]], the museum showcases unique burial practices from [[Southern Kyushu]], such as [[underground horizontal chamber tombs]] specific to the Saitobaru Kofun Group. Other exhibits delve into the [[Hayato people]] of the [[Asuka period|Asuka]] and [[Nara period]]s, who were considered [[barbarians]] under the [[Ritsuryō system]].
The museum conducts periodic special exhibitions, archaeology lectures, workshops, and experiential learning events. A unique "ever-changing exhibition" concept replaces the traditional idea of "permanent exhibitions," ensuring new information is continuously displayed<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Kitagō|first=Yasumichi|year=2004|title=Abolishing Permanent Exhibitions|journal=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Column|volume=7|pages=1-2|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/collumn/007.pdf|format=PDF|ref=harv}}</ref>.
Universal design principles enhance accessibility, including tactile signs and displays for visitors with visual or hearing impairments. The "hands-on exhibits" allow direct interaction with artifacts to promote experiential learning<ref>{{Cite web|title=Universal Design at the Saitobaru Archaeological Museum|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/universal.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>. The exhibit areas are intentionally designed without fixed routes or pathways.
== Facilities == [[File:Saitobaru-kofungun_yokoana-bogun.JPG|thumb|280px|Sakemotonoue Underground Chamber Tomb Group Preservation Building (2013)]] [[File:Saitobaru_Konohanakan.jpg|thumb|280px|Konohana Hall (2019)]] * Ancient Living Experience Center * Saitobaru Guidance Center "Konohana Hall" * Sakemotonoue Underground Chamber Tomb Preservation Building * Saitobaru No.111 Tomb and No.4 [[Underground Horizontal Chamber Tomb]] Preservation Facility
== Visitor Information == * Admission: Free * Opening Hours: 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM * Closed: Mondays, the day following public holidays, and year-end/New Year holidays (December 28 to [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[Category:Miyazaki Prefectural Saitobaru Archaeological Museum]]January 4) * Parking: 100 spaces for regular vehicles, 8 for large buses, and 8 wheelchair-accessible spaces<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Visitor Information|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/usage.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>
== Access == * **Bus**: From [[Miyako City]], board the "Saito Bus Center (via Saitobaru Archaeological Museum)" route (approximately 70 minutes). Alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." * **Car**: Approximately 40 minutes from [[Miyazaki City]] via [[National Route 219]] or 10 minutes from the [[Nishitō Interchange]] on the [[Higashikyushu Expressway]]<ref name=":0" />.

The Saitobaru Kofun Group, located in central Miyazaki on the Saitobaru Plateau, is a massive collection of 319 [[kofun]] (burial mounds), including the imperial tomb references [[Osahozuka Kofun]] and [[Mesahozuka Kofun]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=Overview of the Saitobaru Kofun Group|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/guidance.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>. Opened on April 17, 2004, the Saitobaru Archaeological Museum was established to house, preserve, and display archaeological materials from the kofun group. The museum also explores various archaeological themes from the [[Kofun period]] and beyond<ref>{{Cite web|title=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Exhibition Archives|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/exhibition.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>.
=== Features ===
In addition to exhibits covering the Kofun period across the [[Japanese archipelago]], the museum showcases unique burial practices from [[Southern Kyushu]], such as [[underground horizontal chamber tombs]] specific to the Saitobaru Kofun Group. Other exhibits delve into the [[Hayato people]] of the [[Asuka period|Asuka]] and [[Nara period]]s, who were considered [[barbarians]] under the [[Ritsuryō system]].
The museum conducts periodic special exhibitions, archaeology lectures, workshops, and experiential learning events. A unique "ever-changing exhibition" concept replaces the traditional idea of "permanent exhibitions," ensuring new information is continuously displayed<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Kitagō|first=Yasumichi|year=2004|title=Abolishing Permanent Exhibitions|journal=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Column|volume=7|pages=1-2|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/collumn/007.pdf|format=PDF|ref=harv}}</ref>.
Universal design principles enhance accessibility, including tactile signs and displays for visitors with visual or hearing impairments. The "hands-on exhibits" allow direct interaction with artifacts to promote experiential learning<ref>{{Cite web|title=Universal Design at the Saitobaru Archaeological Museum|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/universal.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>. The exhibit areas are intentionally designed without fixed routes or pathways.
== Facilities == [[File:Saitobaru-kofungun_yokoana-bogun.JPG|thumb|280px|Sakemotonoue Underground Chamber Tomb Group Preservation Building (2013)]] [[File:Saitobaru_Konohanakan.jpg|thumb|280px|Konohana Hall (2019)]] * Ancient Living Experience Center * Saitobaru Guidance Center "Konohana Hall" * Sakemotonoue Underground Chamber Tomb Preservation Building * Saitobaru No.111 Tomb and No.4 [[Underground Horizontal Chamber Tomb]] Preservation Facility
== Visitor Information == * Admission: Free * Opening Hours: 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM * Closed: Mondays, the day following public holidays, and year-end/New Year holidays (December 28 to [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Miyazaki Prefectural Saitobaru Archaeological Museum-qq]] [[Category:Miyazaki Prefectural Saitobaru Archaeological Museum]]January 4) * Parking: 100 spaces for regular vehicles, 8 for large buses, and 8 wheelchair-accessible spaces<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Visitor Information|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/usage.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>
== Access == * **Bus**: From [[Miyako City]], board the "Saito Bus Center (via Saitobaru Archaeological Museum)" route (approximately 70 minutes). Alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." * **Car**: Approximately 40 minutes from [[Miyazaki City]] via [[National Route 219]] or 10 minutes from the [[Nishitō Interchange]] on the [[Higashikyushu Expressway]]<ref name=":0" />.

{{Museum | Name = Miyazaki Prefectural Saitobaru Archaeological Museum | native_name = The Saitobaru Archaeological Museum of Miyazaki Prefecture | native_name_lang = en | imagesize = 280px | image = File:Saitobaru Archaeology Museum.jpg | image_caption = Front entrance | official_name = 宮崎県立西都原考古博物館 | alias = | predecessor = Saitobaru Resource Center | field = History, Archaeology, Saitobaru Kofun Group | collection_size = | visitors = | director = | curators = | research_staff = | operated_by = [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] | management = | annual_budget = | building_design = Reinforced concrete, 4 stories (1 basement, 3 above ground) | total_floor_area = 6,678.63 m² | opening_date = April 17, 2004 (Heisei 16) | closing_date = | postal_code = 881-0005 | address = Miyake Aza Saitobaru Nishi 5670, Saito City, Miyazaki Prefecture | latitude_deg = 32 | latitude_min = 07 | latitude_sec = 31.3 | longitude_deg = 131 | longitude_min = 22 | longitude_sec = 56.6 | access = | website = [http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/index.html Official website] }}
'''Miyazaki Prefectural Saitobaru Archaeological Museum''' (宮崎県立西都原考古博物館, ''Miyazaki Kenritsu Saitobaru Kōko Hakubutsukan'') is a public [[archaeological museum]] located in Miyake Aza Saitobaru Nishi, [[Saito City]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]], Japan. Situated near the [[Special Historic Site]]-designated [[Saitobaru Kofun Group]], the museum primarily exhibits materials related to the kofun group while hosting events on [[archaeology]] and [[historical sites]] throughout Miyazaki Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The Saitobaru Kofun Group, located in central Miyazaki on the Saitobaru Plateau, is a massive collection of 319 [[kofun]] (burial mounds), including the imperial tomb references [[Osahozuka Kofun]] and [[Mesahozuka Kofun]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=Overview of the Saitobaru Kofun Group|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/guidance.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>. Opened on April 17, 2004, the Saitobaru Archaeological Museum was established to house, preserve, and display archaeological materials from the kofun group. The museum also explores various archaeological themes from the [[Kofun period]] and beyond<ref>{{Cite web|title=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Exhibition Archives|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/exhibition.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>.
=== Features ===
In addition to exhibits covering the Kofun period across the [[Japanese archipelago]], the museum showcases unique burial practices from [[Southern Kyushu]], such as [[underground horizontal chamber tombs]] specific to the Saitobaru Kofun Group. Other exhibits delve into the [[Hayato people]] of the [[Asuka period|Asuka]] and [[Nara period]]s, who were considered [[barbarians]] under the [[Ritsuryō system]].
The museum conducts periodic special exhibitions, archaeology lectures, workshops, and experiential learning events. A unique "ever-changing exhibition" concept replaces the traditional idea of "permanent exhibitions," ensuring new information is continuously displayed<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Kitagō|first=Yasumichi|year=2004|title=Abolishing Permanent Exhibitions|journal=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Column|volume=7|pages=1-2|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/collumn/007.pdf|format=PDF|ref=harv}}</ref>.
Universal design principles enhance accessibility, including tactile signs and displays for visitors with visual or hearing impairments. The "hands-on exhibits" allow direct interaction with artifacts to promote experiential learning<ref>{{Cite web|title=Universal Design at the Saitobaru Archaeological Museum|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/universal.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>. The exhibit areas are intentionally designed without fixed routes or pathways.
== Facilities == [[File:Saitobaru-kofungun_yokoana-bogun.JPG|thumb|280px|Sakemotonoue Underground Chamber Tomb Group Preservation Building (2013)]] [[File:Saitobaru_Konohanakan.jpg|thumb|280px|Konohana Hall (2019)]] * Ancient Living Experience Center * Saitobaru Guidance Center "Konohana Hall" * Sakemotonoue Underground Chamber Tomb Preservation Building * Saitobaru No.111 Tomb and No.4 [[Underground Horizontal Chamber Tomb]] Preservation Facility
== Visitor Information == * Admission: Free * Opening Hours: 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM * Closed: Mondays, the day following public holidays, and year-end/New Year holidays (December 28 to [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Miyazaki Prefectural Saitobaru Archaeological Museum-qq]] [[Category:Miyazaki Prefectural Saitobaru Archaeological Museum]]January 4) * Parking: 100 spaces for regular vehicles, 8 for large buses, and 8 wheelchair-accessible spaces<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Visitor Information|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/usage.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>
== Access == * **Bus**: From [[Miyako City]], board the "Saito Bus Center (via Saitobaru Archaeological Museum)" route (approximately 70 minutes). Alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." * **Car**: Approximately 40 minutes from [[Miyazaki City]] via [[National Route 219]] or 10 minutes from the [[Nishitō Interchange]] on the [[Higashikyushu Expressway]]<ref name=":0" />.

The "hands-on exhibits" allow direct interaction with artifacts to promote experiential learning<ref>{{Cite web|title=Universal Design at the Saitobaru Archaeological Museum|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/universal.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>. The exhibit areas are intentionally designed without fixed routes or pathways.

== Visitor Information == * Admission: Free * Opening Hours: 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM * Closed: Mondays, the day following public holidays, and year-end/New Year holidays (December 28 to [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]]
[[Category:qq deprecated open]]<!--Miyazaki Prefectural Saitobaru Archaeological Museum-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Miyazaki Prefectural Saitobaru Archaeological Museum]]January 4) * Parking: 100 spaces for regular vehicles, 8 for large buses, and 8 wheelchair-accessible spaces<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Visitor Information|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/usage.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>
== Access == * **Bus**: From [[Miyako City]], board the "Saito Bus Center (via Saitobaru Archaeological Museum)" route (approximately 70 minutes). Alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." * **Car**: Approximately 40 minutes from [[Miyazaki City]] via [[National Route 219]] or 10 minutes from the [[Nishitō Interchange]] on the [[Higashikyushu Expressway]]<ref name=":0" />.

{{Museum | Name = Miyazaki Prefectural Saitobaru Archaeological Museum | native_name = The Saitobaru Archaeological Museum of Miyazaki Prefecture | native_name_lang = en | imagesize = 280px | image = File:Saitobaru Archaeology Museum.jpg | image_caption = Front entrance | official_name = 宮崎県立西都原考古博物館 | alias = | predecessor = Saitobaru Resource Center | field = History, Archaeology, Saitobaru Kofun Group | collection_size = | visitors = | director = | curators = | research_staff = | operated_by = [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] | management = | annual_budget = | building_design = Reinforced concrete, 4 stories (1 basement, 3 above ground) | total_floor_area = 6,678.63 m² | opening_date = April 17, 2004 (Heisei 16) | closing_date = | postal_code = 881-0005 | address = Miyake Aza Saitobaru Nishi 5670, Saito City, Miyazaki Prefecture | latitude_deg = 32 | latitude_min = 07 | latitude_sec = 31.3 | longitude_deg = 131 | longitude_min = 22 | longitude_sec = 56.6 | access = | website = [http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/index.html Official website] }}
'''Miyazaki Prefectural Saitobaru Archaeological Museum''' (宮崎県立西都原考古博物館, ''Miyazaki Kenritsu Saitobaru Kōko Hakubutsukan'') is a public [[archaeological museum]] located in Miyake Aza Saitobaru Nishi, [[Saito City]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]], Japan. Situated near the [[Special Historic Site]]-designated [[Saitobaru Kofun Group]], the museum primarily exhibits materials related to the kofun group while hosting events on [[archaeology]] and [[historical sites]] throughout Miyazaki Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The Saitobaru Kofun Group, located in central Miyazaki on the Saitobaru Plateau, is a massive collection of 319 [[kofun]] (burial mounds), including the imperial tomb references [[Osahozuka Kofun]] and [[Mesahozuka Kofun]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=Overview of the Saitobaru Kofun Group|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/guidance.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>. Opened on April 17, 2004, the Saitobaru Archaeological Museum was established to house, preserve, and display archaeological materials from the kofun group. The museum also explores various archaeological themes from the [[Kofun period]] and beyond<ref>{{Cite web|title=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Exhibition Archives|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/exhibition.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>.
=== Features ===
In addition to exhibits covering the Kofun period across the [[Japanese archipelago]], the museum showcases unique burial practices from [[Southern Kyushu]], such as [[underground horizontal chamber tombs]] specific to the Saitobaru Kofun Group. Other exhibits delve into the [[Hayato people]] of the [[Asuka period|Asuka]] and [[Nara period]]s, who were considered [[barbarians]] under the [[Ritsuryō system]].
The museum conducts periodic special exhibitions, archaeology lectures, workshops, and experiential learning events. A unique "ever-changing exhibition" concept replaces the traditional idea of "permanent exhibitions," ensuring new information is continuously displayed<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Kitagō|first=Yasumichi|year=2004|title=Abolishing Permanent Exhibitions|journal=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Column|volume=7|pages=1-2|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/collumn/007.pdf|format=PDF|ref=harv}}</ref>.
Universal design principles enhance accessibility, including tactile signs and displays for visitors with visual or hearing impairments. The "hands-on exhibits" allow direct interaction with artifacts to promote experiential learning<ref>{{Cite web|title=Universal Design at the Saitobaru Archaeological Museum|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/universal.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>. The exhibit areas are intentionally designed without fixed routes or pathways.
== Facilities == [[File:Saitobaru-kofungun_yokoana-bogun.JPG|thumb|280px|Sakemotonoue Underground Chamber Tomb Group Preservation Building (2013)]] [[File:Saitobaru_Konohanakan.jpg|thumb|280px|Konohana Hall (2019)]] * Ancient Living Experience Center * Saitobaru Guidance Center "Konohana Hall" * Sakemotonoue Underground Chamber Tomb Preservation Building * Saitobaru No.111 Tomb and No.4 [[Underground Horizontal Chamber Tomb]] Preservation Facility
== Visitor Information == * Admission: Free * Opening Hours: 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM * Closed: Mondays, the day following public holidays, and year-end/New Year holidays (December 28 to [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:Tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyazaki Prefectural Saitobaru Archaeological Museum]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]]<!--Miyazaki Prefectural Saitobaru Archaeological Museum-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Miyazaki Prefectural Saitobaru Archaeological Museum]]January 4) * Parking: 100 spaces for regular vehicles, 8 for large buses, and 8 wheelchair-accessible spaces<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Visitor Information|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/usage.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>
== Access == * **Bus**: From [[Miyako City]], board the "Saito Bus Center (via Saitobaru Archaeological Museum)" route (approximately 70 minutes). Alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." * **Car**: Approximately 40 minutes from [[Miyazaki City]] via [[National Route 219]] or 10 minutes from the [[Nishitō Interchange]] on the [[Higashikyushu Expressway]]<ref name=":0" />.

revision.text:
{{Museum | Name = Miyazaki Prefectural Saitobaru Archaeological Museum | native_name = The Saitobaru Archaeological Museum of Miyazaki Prefecture | native_name_lang = en | imagesize = 280px | image = File:Saitobaru Archaeology Museum.jpg | image_caption = Front entrance | official_name = 宮崎県立西都原考古博物館 | alias = | predecessor = Saitobaru Resource Center | field = History, Archaeology, Saitobaru Kofun Group | collection_size = | visitors = | director = | curators = | research_staff = | operated_by = [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] | management = | annual_budget = | building_design = Reinforced concrete, 4 stories (1 basement, 3 above ground) | total_floor_area = 6,678.63 m² | opening_date = April 17, 2004 (Heisei 16) | closing_date = | postal_code = 881-0005 | address = Miyake Aza Saitobaru Nishi 5670, Saito City, Miyazaki Prefecture | latitude_deg = 32 | latitude_min = 07 | latitude_sec = 31.3 | longitude_deg = 131 | longitude_min = 22 | longitude_sec = 56.6 | access = | website = [http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/index.html Official website] }}
'''Miyazaki Prefectural Saitobaru Archaeological Museum''' (宮崎県立西都原考古博物館, ''Miyazaki Kenritsu Saitobaru Kōko Hakubutsukan'') is a public [[archaeological museum]] located in Miyake Aza Saitobaru Nishi, [[Saito City]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]], Japan. Situated near the [[Special Historic Site]]-designated [[Saitobaru Kofun Group]], the museum primarily exhibits materials related to the kofun group while hosting events on [[archaeology]] and [[historical sites]] throughout Miyazaki Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The Saitobaru Kofun Group, located in central Miyazaki on the Saitobaru Plateau, is a massive collection of 319 [[kofun]] (Kofuns), including the imperial tomb references [[Osahozuka Kofun]] and [[Mesahozuka Kofun]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=Overview of the Saitobaru Kofun Group|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/guidance.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>. Opened on April 17, 2004, the Saitobaru Archaeological Museum was established to house, preserve, and display archaeological materials from the kofun group. The museum also explores various archaeological themes from the [[Kofun period]] and beyond<ref>{{Cite web|title=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Exhibition Archives|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/exhibition.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>.
=== Features ===
In addition to exhibits covering the Kofun period across the [[Japanese archipelago]], the museum showcases unique burial practices from [[Southern Kyushu]], such as [[underground horizontal chamber tombs]] specific to the Saitobaru Kofun Group. Other exhibits delve into the [[Hayato people]] of the [[Asuka period|Asuka]] and [[Nara period]]s, who were considered [[barbarians]] under the [[Ritsuryō system]].
The museum conducts periodic special exhibitions, archaeology lectures, workshops, and experiential learning events. A unique "ever-changing exhibition" concept replaces the traditional idea of "permanent exhibitions," ensuring new information is continuously displayed<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Kitagō|first=Yasumichi|year=2004|title=Abolishing Permanent Exhibitions|journal=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Column|volume=7|pages=1-2|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/collumn/007.pdf|format=PDF|ref=harv}}</ref>.
Universal design principles enhance accessibility, including tactile signs and displays for visitors with visual or hearing impairments. The "hands-on exhibits" allow direct interaction with artifacts to promote experiential learning<ref>{{Cite web|title=Universal Design at the Saitobaru Archaeological Museum|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/universal.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>. The exhibit areas are intentionally designed without fixed routes or pathways.
== Facilities == [[File:Saitobaru-kofungun_yokoana-bogun.JPG|thumb|280px|Sakemotonoue Underground Chamber Tomb Group Preservation Building (2013)]] [[File:Saitobaru_Konohanakan.jpg|thumb|280px|Konohana Hall (2019)]] * Ancient Living Experience Center * Saitobaru Guidance Center "Konohana Hall" * Sakemotonoue Underground Chamber Tomb Preservation Building * Saitobaru No.111 Tomb and No.4 [[Underground Horizontal Chamber Tomb]] Preservation Facility
== Visitor Information == * Admission: Free * Opening Hours: 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM * Closed: Mondays, the day following public holidays, and year-end/New Year holidays (December 28 to [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:Tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyazaki Prefectural Saitobaru Archaeological Museum]][[Category:qq]] <!--Miyazaki Prefectural Saitobaru Archaeological Museum--> [[Category:Miyazaki Prefectural Saitobaru Archaeological Museum]]January 4) * Parking: 100 spaces for regular vehicles, 8 for large buses, and 8 wheelchair-accessible spaces<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Visitor Information|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/usage.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>
== Access == * **Bus**: From [[Miyako City]], board the "Saito Bus Center (via Saitobaru Archaeological Museum)" route (approximately 70 minutes). Alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." * **Car**: Approximately 40 minutes from [[Miyazaki City]] via [[National Route 219]] or 10 minutes from the [[Nishitō Interchange]] on the [[Higashikyushu Expressway]]<ref name=":0" />.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Citation help}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}}
== External Links == * [https://www.saito-kanko.jp/sightseeing/%e5%ae%ae%e5%b4%8e%e7%9c%8c%e7%ab%8b%e8%a5%bf%e9%83%bd%e5%8e%9f%e8%80%83%e5%8f%a4%e5%8d%9a%e7%89%a9%e9%a4%a8 Saito City Tourism Association] * [http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/index.html Official website]

Alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." * **Car**: Approximately 40 minutes from [[Miyazaki City]] via [[National Route 219]] or 10 minutes from the [[Nishitō Interchange]] on the [[Higashikyushu Expressway]]<ref name=":0" />.

{{Museum | Name = Miyazaki Prefectural Saitobaru Archaeological Museum | native_name = The Saitobaru Archaeological Museum of Miyazaki Prefecture | native_name_lang = en | imagesize = 280px | image = File:Saitobaru Archaeology Museum.jpg | image_caption = Front entrance | official_name = 宮崎県立西都原考古博物館 | alias = | predecessor = Saitobaru Resource Center | field = History, Archaeology, Saitobaru Kofun Group | collection_size = | visitors = | director = | curators = | research_staff = | operated_by = [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] | management = | annual_budget = | building_design = Reinforced concrete, 4 stories (1 basement, 3 above ground) | total_floor_area = 6,678.63 m² | opening_date = April 17, 2004 (Heisei 16) | closing_date = | postal_code = 881-0005 | address = Miyake Aza Saitobaru Nishi 5670, Saito City, Miyazaki Prefecture | latitude_deg = 32 | latitude_min = 07 | latitude_sec = 31.3 | longitude_deg = 131 | longitude_min = 22 | longitude_sec = 56.6 | access = | website = [http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/index.html Official website] }}
'''Miyazaki Prefectural Saitobaru Archaeological Museum''' (宮崎県立西都原考古博物館, ''Miyazaki Kenritsu Saitobaru Kōko Hakubutsukan'') is a public [[archaeological museum]] located in Miyake Aza Saitobaru Nishi, [[Saito City]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]], Japan. Situated near the [[Special Historic Site]]-designated [[Saitobaru Kofun Group]], the museum primarily exhibits materials related to the kofun group while hosting events on [[archaeology]] and [[historical sites]] throughout Miyazaki Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The Saitobaru Kofun Group, located in central Miyazaki on the Saitobaru Plateau, is a massive collection of 319 [[kofun]] (Kofuns), including the imperial tomb references [[Osahozuka Kofun]] and [[Mesahozuka Kofun]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=Overview of the Saitobaru Kofun Group|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/guidance.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>. Opened on April 17, 2004, the Saitobaru Archaeological Museum was established to house, preserve, and display archaeological materials from the kofun group. The museum also explores various archaeological themes from the [[Kofun period]] and beyond<ref>{{Cite web|title=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Exhibition Archives|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/exhibition.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>.
=== Features ===
In addition to exhibits covering the Kofun period across the [[Japanese archipelago]], the museum showcases unique burial practices from [[Southern Kyushu]], such as [[underground horizontal chamber tombs]] specific to the Saitobaru Kofun Group. Other exhibits delve into the [[Hayato people]] of the [[Asuka period|Asuka]] and [[Nara period]]s, who were considered [[barbarians]] under the [[Ritsuryō system]].
The museum conducts periodic special exhibitions, archaeology lectures, workshops, and experiential learning events. A unique "ever-changing exhibition" concept replaces the traditional idea of "permanent exhibitions," ensuring new information is continuously displayed<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Kitagō|first=Yasumichi|year=2004|title=Abolishing Permanent Exhibitions|journal=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Column|volume=7|pages=1-2|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/collumn/007.pdf|format=PDF|ref=harv}}</ref>.
Universal design principles enhance accessibility, including tactile signs and displays for visitors with visual or hearing impairments. The "hands-on exhibits" allow direct interaction with artifacts to promote experiential learning<ref>{{Cite web|title=Universal Design at the Saitobaru Archaeological Museum|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/universal.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>. The exhibit areas are intentionally designed without fixed routes or pathways.
== Facilities == [[File:Saitobaru-kofungun_yokoana-bogun.JPG|thumb|280px|Sakemotonoue Underground Chamber Tomb Group Preservation Building (2013)]] [[File:Saitobaru_Konohanakan.jpg|thumb|280px|Konohana Hall (2019)]] * Ancient Living Experience Center * Saitobaru Guidance Center "Konohana Hall" * Sakemotonoue Underground Chamber Tomb Preservation Building * Saitobaru No.111 Tomb and No.4 [[Underground Horizontal Chamber Tomb]] Preservation Facility
== Visitor Information == * Admission: Free * Opening Hours: 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM * Closed: Mondays, the day following public holidays, and year-end/New Year holidays (December 28 to [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:Tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Miyazaki Prefectural Saitobaru Archaeological Museum]][[Category:11]] <!--Miyazaki Prefectural Saitobaru Archaeological Museum--> [[Category:Miyazaki Prefectural Saitobaru Archaeological Museum]]January 4) * Parking: 100 spaces for regular vehicles, 8 for large buses, and 8 wheelchair-accessible spaces<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Visitor Information|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/usage.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>
== Access == * **Bus**: From [[Miyako City]], board the "Saito Bus Center (via Saitobaru Archaeological Museum)" route (approximately 70 minutes). Alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." * **Car**: Approximately 40 minutes from [[Miyazaki City]] via [[National Route 219]] or 10 minutes from the [[Nishitō Interchange]] on the [[Higashikyushu Expressway]]<ref name=":0" />.

'''Miyazaki Prefectural Saitobaru Archaeological Museum''' (宮崎県立西都原考古博物館, ''Miyazaki Kenritsu Saitobaru Kōko Hakubutsukan'') is a public [[archaeological museum]] located in Miyake Aza Saitobaru Nishi, [[Saito City]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]], Japan. Situated near the [[Special Historic Site]]-designated [[Saitobaru Kofun Group]], the museum primarily exhibits materials related to the kofun group while hosting events on [[archaeology]] and [[historical sites]] throughout Miyazaki Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The Saitobaru Kofun Group, located in central Miyazaki on the Saitobaru Plateau, is a massive collection of 319 [[kofun]] (Kofuns), including the imperial tomb references [[Osahozuka Kofun]] and [[Mesahozuka Kofun]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=Overview of the Saitobaru Kofun Group|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/guidance.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>. Opened on April 17, 2004, the Saitobaru Archaeological Museum was established to house, preserve, and display archaeological materials from the kofun group. The museum also explores various archaeological themes from the [[Kofun period]] and beyond<ref>{{Cite web|title=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Exhibition Archives|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/exhibition.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>.
=== Features ===
In addition to exhibits covering the Kofun period across the [[Japanese archipelago]], the museum showcases unique burial practices from [[Southern Kyushu]], such as [[underground horizontal chamber tombs]] specific to the Saitobaru Kofun Group. Other exhibits delve into the [[Hayato people]] of the [[Asuka period|Asuka]] and [[Nara period]]s, who were considered [[barbarians]] under the [[Ritsuryō system]].
The museum conducts periodic special exhibitions, archaeology lectures, workshops, and experiential learning events. A unique "ever-changing exhibition" concept replaces the traditional idea of "permanent exhibitions," ensuring new information is continuously displayed<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Kitagō|first=Yasumichi|year=2004|title=Abolishing Permanent Exhibitions|journal=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Column|volume=7|pages=1-2|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/collumn/007.pdf|format=PDF|ref=harv}}</ref>.
Universal design principles enhance accessibility, including tactile signs and displays for visitors with visual or hearing impairments. The "hands-on exhibits" allow direct interaction with artifacts to promote experiential learning<ref>{{Cite web|title=Universal Design at the Saitobaru Archaeological Museum|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/universal.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>. The exhibit areas are intentionally designed without fixed routes or pathways.
== Facilities == [[File:Saitobaru-kofungun_yokoana-bogun.JPG|thumb|280px|Sakemotonoue Underground Chamber Tomb Group Preservation Building (2013)]] [[File:Saitobaru_Konohanakan.jpg|thumb|280px|Konohana Hall (2019)]] * Ancient Living Experience Center * Saitobaru Guidance Center "Konohana Hall" * Sakemotonoue Underground Chamber Tomb Preservation Building * Saitobaru No.111 Tomb and No.4 [[Underground Horizontal Chamber Tomb]] Preservation Facility
== Visitor Information == * Admission: Free * Opening Hours: 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM * Closed: Mondays, the day following public holidays, and year-end/New Year holidays (December 28 to [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]][[Category:11]][[sn:Miyazaki Prefectural Saitobaru Archaeological Museum]][[Category:11]] <!--Miyazaki Prefectural Saitobaru Archaeological Museum--> [[Category:Miyazaki Prefectural Saitobaru Archaeological Museum]]January 4) * Parking: 100 spaces for regular vehicles, 8 for large buses, and 8 wheelchair-accessible spaces<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Visitor Information|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/usage.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>
== Access == * **Bus**: From [[Miyako City]], board the "Saito Bus Center (via Saitobaru Archaeological Museum)" route (approximately 70 minutes). Alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." * **Car**: Approximately 40 minutes from [[Miyazaki City]] via [[National Route 219]] or 10 minutes from the [[Nishitō Interchange]] on the [[Higashikyushu Expressway]]<ref name=":0" />.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Citation help}}
