<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Saitobaru Archaeological Museum of Miyazaki Prefecture]][[ja:宮崎県立西都原考古博物館]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q68478310]] Wikidata name: Saitobaru Archaeological Museum of Miyazaki Prefecture [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Museum | Name = Miyazaki Prefectural Saitobaru Archaeological Museum | native_name = The Saitobaru Archaeological Museum of Miyazaki Prefecture | native_name_lang = en | imagesize = 280px | image = File:Saitobaru Archaeology Museum.jpg | image_caption = Front entrance | official_name = 宮崎県立西都原考古博物館 | alias = | predecessor = Saitobaru Resource Center | field = History, Archaeology, Saitobaru Kofun Group | collection_size = | visitors = | director = | curators = | research_staff = | operated_by = [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]] | management = | annual_budget = | building_design = Reinforced concrete, 4 stories (1 basement, 3 above ground) | total_floor_area = 6,678.63 m² | opening_date = April 17, 2004 (Heisei 16) | closing_date = | postal_code = 881-0005 | address = Miyake Aza Saitobaru Nishi 5670, Saito City, Miyazaki Prefecture | latitude_deg = 32 | latitude_min = 07 | latitude_sec = 31.3 | longitude_deg = 131 | longitude_min = 22 | longitude_sec = 56.6 | access = | website = [http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/index.html Official website] }}
'''Miyazaki Prefectural Saitobaru Archaeological Museum''' (宮崎県立西都原考古博物館, ''Miyazaki Kenritsu Saitobaru Kōko Hakubutsukan'') is a public [[:en:Archaeological museum|Archaeological museum]] located in Miyake Aza Saitobaru Nishi, [[Saito City]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]], Japan. Situated near the [[:en:Special Historic Site|Special Historic Site]]-designated [[Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]], the museum primarily exhibits materials related to the kofun group while hosting events on [[:en:Archaeology|Archaeology]] and [[historical sites]] throughout Miyazaki Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The Saitobaru Kofun Group, located in central Miyazaki on the Saitobaru Plateau, is a massive collection of 319 [[kofun]] (Kofuns), including the imperial tomb references [[Ozasahozuka Kofun|Osahozuka Kofun]] and [[Mesahozuka Kofun]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=Overview of the Saitobaru Kofun Group|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/guidance.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>. Opened on April 17, 2004, the Saitobaru Archaeological Museum was established to house, preserve, and display archaeological materials from the kofun group. The museum also explores various archaeological themes from the [[Kofun period]] and beyond<ref>{{Cite web|title=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Exhibition Archives|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/exhibition.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>.
=== Features ===
In addition to exhibits covering the Kofun period across the [[Japanese archipelago]], the museum showcases unique burial practices from [[:en:Southern Kyushu|Southern Kyushu]], such as [[underground horizontal chamber tombs]] specific to the Saitobaru Kofun Group. Other exhibits delve into the [[:en:Hayato people|Hayato people]] of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka]] and [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]s, who were considered [[:en:barbarians|barbarians]] under the [[Ritsuryō system]].
The museum conducts periodic special exhibitions, archaeology lectures, workshops, and experiential learning events. A unique "ever-changing exhibition" concept replaces the traditional idea of "permanent exhibitions," ensuring new information is continuously displayed<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Kitagō|first=Yasumichi|year=2004|title=Abolishing Permanent Exhibitions|journal=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Column|volume=7|pages=1-2|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/collumn/007.pdf|format=PDF|ref=harv}}</ref>.
Universal design principles enhance accessibility, including tactile signs and displays for visitors with visual or hearing impairments. The "hands-on exhibits" allow direct interaction with artifacts to promote experiential learning<ref>{{Cite web|title=Universal Design at the Saitobaru Archaeological Museum|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/universal.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>. The exhibit areas are intentionally designed without fixed routes or pathways.
== Facilities == [[File:Saitobaru-kofungun_yokoana-bogun.JPG|thumb|280px|Sakemotonoue Underground Chamber Tomb Group Preservation Building (2013)]] [[File:Saitobaru_Konohanakan.jpg|thumb|280px|Konohana Hall (2019)]] * Ancient Living Experience Center * Saitobaru Guidance Center "Konohana Hall" * Sakemotonoue Underground Chamber Tomb Preservation Building * Saitobaru No.111 Tomb and No.4 [[Underground Horizontal Chamber Tomb]] Preservation Facility
== Visitor Information == * Admission: Free * Opening Hours: 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM * Closed: Mondays, the day following public holidays, and year-end/New Year holidays (December 28 to
January 4) * Parking: 100 spaces for regular vehicles, 8 for large buses, and 8 wheelchair-accessible spaces<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Visitor Information|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/usage.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>
== Access == * **Bus**: From [[:en:Miyako City|Miyako City]], board the "Saito Bus Center (via Saitobaru Archaeological Museum)" route (approximately 70 minutes). Alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." * **Car**: Approximately 40 minutes from [[:en:Miyazaki City|Miyazaki City]] via [[:en:National Route 219|National Route 219]] or 10 minutes from the [[Nishitō Interchange]] on the [[:en:Higashikyushu Expressway|Higashikyushu Expressway]]<ref name=":0" />.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Citation help}}
== Related Articles == * [[:en:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]]
== External Links == * [https://www.saito-kanko.jp/sightseeing/%e5%ae%ae%e5%b4%8e%e7%9c%8c%e7%ab%8b%e8%a5%bf%e9%83%bd%e5%8e%9f%e8%80%83%e5%8f%a4%e5%8d%9a%e7%89%a9%e9%a4%a8 Saito City Tourism Association] * [http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/index.html Official website]
{{Museum Law Institutions}}

Situated near the [[:en:Special Historic Site|Special Historic Site]]-designated [[Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]], the museum primarily exhibits materials related to the kofun group while hosting events on [[:en:Archaeology|Archaeology]] and [[historical sites]] throughout Miyazaki Prefecture.

The Saitobaru Kofun Group, located in central Miyazaki on the Saitobaru Plateau, is a massive collection of 319 [[kofun]] (Kofuns), including the imperial tomb references [[Ozasahozuka Kofun|Osahozuka Kofun]] and [[Mesahozuka Kofun]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=Overview of the Saitobaru Kofun Group|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/guidance.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>. Opened on April 17, 2004, the Saitobaru Archaeological Museum was established to house, preserve, and display archaeological materials from the kofun group. The museum also explores various archaeological themes from the [[Kofun period]] and beyond<ref>{{Cite web|title=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Exhibition Archives|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/exhibition.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>.
=== Features ===
In addition to exhibits covering the Kofun period across the [[Japanese archipelago]], the museum showcases unique burial practices from [[:en:Southern Kyushu|Southern Kyushu]], such as [[underground horizontal chamber tombs]] specific to the Saitobaru Kofun Group. Other exhibits delve into the [[:en:Hayato people|Hayato people]] of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka]] and [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]s, who were considered [[:en:barbarians|barbarians]] under the [[Ritsuryō system]].
The museum conducts periodic special exhibitions, archaeology lectures, workshops, and experiential learning events. A unique "ever-changing exhibition" concept replaces the traditional idea of "permanent exhibitions," ensuring new information is continuously displayed<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Kitagō|first=Yasumichi|year=2004|title=Abolishing Permanent Exhibitions|journal=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Column|volume=7|pages=1-2|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/collumn/007.pdf|format=PDF|ref=harv}}</ref>.
Universal design principles enhance accessibility, including tactile signs and displays for visitors with visual or hearing impairments. The "hands-on exhibits" allow direct interaction with artifacts to promote experiential learning<ref>{{Cite web|title=Universal Design at the Saitobaru Archaeological Museum|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/universal.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>. The exhibit areas are intentionally designed without fixed routes or pathways.
== Facilities == [[File:Saitobaru-kofungun_yokoana-bogun.JPG|thumb|280px|Sakemotonoue Underground Chamber Tomb Group Preservation Building (2013)]] [[File:Saitobaru_Konohanakan.jpg|thumb|280px|Konohana Hall (2019)]] * Ancient Living Experience Center * Saitobaru Guidance Center "Konohana Hall" * Sakemotonoue Underground Chamber Tomb Preservation Building * Saitobaru No.111 Tomb and No.4 [[Underground Horizontal Chamber Tomb]] Preservation Facility
== Visitor Information == * Admission: Free * Opening Hours: 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM * Closed: Mondays, the day following public holidays, and year-end/New Year holidays (December 28 to
January 4) * Parking: 100 spaces for regular vehicles, 8 for large buses, and 8 wheelchair-accessible spaces<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Visitor Information|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/usage.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>
== Access == * **Bus**: From [[:en:Miyako City|Miyako City]], board the "Saito Bus Center (via Saitobaru Archaeological Museum)" route (approximately 70 minutes). Alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." * **Car**: Approximately 40 minutes from [[:en:Miyazaki City|Miyazaki City]] via [[:en:National Route 219|National Route 219]] or 10 minutes from the [[Nishitō Interchange]] on the [[:en:Higashikyushu Expressway|Higashikyushu Expressway]]<ref name=":0" />.

'''Miyazaki Prefectural Saitobaru Archaeological Museum''' (宮崎県立西都原考古博物館, ''Miyazaki Kenritsu Saitobaru Kōko Hakubutsukan'') is a public [[:en:Archaeological museum|Archaeological museum]] located in Miyake Aza Saitobaru Nishi, [[Saito City]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]], Japan. Situated near the [[:en:Special Historic Site|Special Historic Site]]-designated [[Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]], the museum primarily exhibits materials related to the kofun group while hosting events on [[:en:Archaeology|Archaeology]] and [[historical sites]] throughout Miyazaki Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The Saitobaru Kofun Group, located in central Miyazaki on the Saitobaru Plateau, is a massive collection of 319 [[kofun]] (Kofuns), including the imperial tomb references [[Ozasahozuka Kofun|Osahozuka Kofun]] and [[Mesahozuka Kofun]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=Overview of the Saitobaru Kofun Group|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/guidance.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>. Opened on April 17, 2004, the Saitobaru Archaeological Museum was established to house, preserve, and display archaeological materials from the kofun group. The museum also explores various archaeological themes from the [[Kofun period]] and beyond<ref>{{Cite web|title=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Exhibition Archives|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/exhibition.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>.
=== Features ===
In addition to exhibits covering the Kofun period across the [[Japanese archipelago]], the museum showcases unique burial practices from [[:en:Southern Kyushu|Southern Kyushu]], such as [[underground horizontal chamber tombs]] specific to the Saitobaru Kofun Group. Other exhibits delve into the [[:en:Hayato people|Hayato people]] of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka]] and [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]s, who were considered [[:en:barbarians|barbarians]] under the [[Ritsuryō system]].
The museum conducts periodic special exhibitions, archaeology lectures, workshops, and experiential learning events. A unique "ever-changing exhibition" concept replaces the traditional idea of "permanent exhibitions," ensuring new information is continuously displayed<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Kitagō|first=Yasumichi|year=2004|title=Abolishing Permanent Exhibitions|journal=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Column|volume=7|pages=1-2|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/collumn/007.pdf|format=PDF|ref=harv}}</ref>.
Universal design principles enhance accessibility, including tactile signs and displays for visitors with visual or hearing impairments. The "hands-on exhibits" allow direct interaction with artifacts to promote experiential learning<ref>{{Cite web|title=Universal Design at the Saitobaru Archaeological Museum|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/universal.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>. == Visitor Information == * Admission: Free * Opening Hours: 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM * Closed: Mondays, the day following public holidays, and year-end/New Year holidays (December 28 to
January 4) * Parking: 100 spaces for regular vehicles, 8 for large buses, and 8 wheelchair-accessible spaces<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Visitor Information|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/usage.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>
== Access == * **Bus**: From [[:en:Miyako City|Miyako City]], board the "Saito Bus Center (via Saitobaru Archaeological Museum)" route (approximately 70 minutes). January 4) * Parking: 100 spaces for regular vehicles, 8 for large buses, and 8 wheelchair-accessible spaces<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Visitor Information|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/usage.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Saitobaru Archaeological Museum of Miyazaki Prefecture]][[ja:宮崎県立西都原考古博物館]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Saitobaru Archaeological Museum of Miyazaki Prefecture]][[ja:宮崎県立西都原考古博物館]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Saitobaru Archaeological Museum of Miyazaki Prefecture]][[ja:宮崎県立西都原考古博物館]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q68478310]] Wikidata name: Saitobaru Archaeological Museum of Miyazaki Prefecture [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Museum | Name = Miyazaki Prefectural Saitobaru Archaeological Museum | native_name = The Saitobaru Archaeological Museum of Miyazaki Prefecture | native_name_lang = en | imagesize = 280px | image = File:Saitobaru Archaeology Museum.jpg | image_caption = Front entrance | official_name = 宮崎県立西都原考古博物館 | alias = | predecessor = Saitobaru Resource Center | field = History, Archaeology, Saitobaru Kofun Group | collection_size = | visitors = | director = | curators = | research_staff = | operated_by = [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]] | management = | annual_budget = | building_design = Reinforced concrete, 4 stories (1 basement, 3 above ground) | total_floor_area = 6,678.63 m² | opening_date = April 17, 2004 (Heisei 16) | closing_date = | postal_code = 881-0005 | address = Miyake Aza Saitobaru Nishi 5670, Saito City, Miyazaki Prefecture | latitude_deg = 32 | latitude_min = 07 | latitude_sec = 31.3 | longitude_deg = 131 | longitude_min = 22 | longitude_sec = 56.6 | access = | website = [http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/index.html Official website] }}
'''Miyazaki Prefectural Saitobaru Archaeological Museum''' (宮崎県立西都原考古博物館, ''Miyazaki Kenritsu Saitobaru Kōko Hakubutsukan'') is a public [[:en:Archaeological museum|Archaeological museum]] located in Miyake Aza Saitobaru Nishi, [[Saito City]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]], Japan. Situated near the [[:en:Special Historic Site|Special Historic Site]]-designated [[Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]], the museum primarily exhibits materials related to the kofun group while hosting events on [[:en:Archaeology|Archaeology]] and [[historical sites]] throughout Miyazaki Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The Saitobaru Kofun Group, located in central Miyazaki on the Saitobaru Plateau, is a massive collection of 319 [[kofun]] (Kofuns), including the imperial tomb references [[Ozasahozuka Kofun|Osahozuka Kofun]] and [[Mesahozuka Kofun]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=Overview of the Saitobaru Kofun Group|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/guidance.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>. Opened on April 17, 2004, the Saitobaru Archaeological Museum was established to house, preserve, and display archaeological materials from the kofun group. The museum also explores various archaeological themes from the [[Kofun period]] and beyond<ref>{{Cite web|title=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Exhibition Archives|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/exhibition.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>.
=== Features ===
In addition to exhibits covering the Kofun period across the [[Japanese archipelago]], the museum showcases unique burial practices from [[:en:Southern Kyushu|Southern Kyushu]], such as [[underground horizontal chamber tombs]] specific to the Saitobaru Kofun Group. Other exhibits delve into the [[:en:Hayato people|Hayato people]] of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka]] and [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]s, who were considered [[:en:barbarians|barbarians]] under the [[Ritsuryō system]].
The museum conducts periodic special exhibitions, archaeology lectures, workshops, and experiential learning events. A unique "ever-changing exhibition" concept replaces the traditional idea of "permanent exhibitions," ensuring new information is continuously displayed<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Kitagō|first=Yasumichi|year=2004|title=Abolishing Permanent Exhibitions|journal=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Column|volume=7|pages=1-2|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/collumn/007.pdf|format=PDF|ref=harv}}</ref>.
Universal design principles enhance accessibility, including tactile signs and displays for visitors with visual or hearing impairments. The "hands-on exhibits" allow direct interaction with artifacts to promote experiential learning<ref>{{Cite web|title=Universal Design at the Saitobaru Archaeological Museum|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/universal.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>. The exhibit areas are intentionally designed without fixed routes or pathways.
== Facilities == [[File:Saitobaru-kofungun_yokoana-bogun.JPG|thumb|280px|Sakemotonoue Underground Chamber Tomb Group Preservation Building (2013)]] [[File:Saitobaru_Konohanakan.jpg|thumb|280px|Konohana Hall (2019)]] * Ancient Living Experience Center * Saitobaru Guidance Center "Konohana Hall" * Sakemotonoue Underground Chamber Tomb Preservation Building * Saitobaru No.111 Tomb and No.4 [[Underground Horizontal Chamber Tomb]] Preservation Facility
== Visitor Information == * Admission: Free * Opening Hours: 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM * Closed: Mondays, the day following public holidays, and year-end/New Year holidays (December 28 to
January 4) * Parking: 100 spaces for regular vehicles, 8 for large buses, and 8 wheelchair-accessible spaces<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Visitor Information|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/usage.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>
== Access == * **Bus**: From [[:en:Miyako City|Miyako City]], board the "Saito Bus Center (via Saitobaru Archaeological Museum)" route (approximately 70 minutes). Alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." * **Car**: Approximately 40 minutes from [[:en:Miyazaki City|Miyazaki City]] via [[:en:National Route 219|National Route 219]] or 10 minutes from the [[Nishitō Interchange]] on the [[:en:Higashikyushu Expressway|Higashikyushu Expressway]]<ref name=":0" />.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Saitobaru Archaeological Museum of Miyazaki Prefecture]][[ja:宮崎県立西都原考古博物館]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Saitobaru Archaeological Museum of Miyazaki Prefecture]][[ja:宮崎県立西都原考古博物館]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Saitobaru Archaeological Museum of Miyazaki Prefecture]][[ja:宮崎県立西都原考古博物館]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q68478310]] Wikidata name: Saitobaru Archaeological Museum of Miyazaki Prefecture [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Museum | Name = Miyazaki Prefectural Saitobaru Archaeological Museum | native_name = The Saitobaru Archaeological Museum of Miyazaki Prefecture | native_name_lang = en | imagesize = 280px | image = File:Saitobaru Archaeology Museum.jpg | image_caption = Front entrance | official_name = 宮崎県立西都原考古博物館 | alias = | predecessor = Saitobaru Resource Center | field = History, Archaeology, Saitobaru Kofun Group | collection_size = | visitors = | director = | curators = | research_staff = | operated_by = [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]] | management = | annual_budget = | building_design = Reinforced concrete, 4 stories (1 basement, 3 above ground) | total_floor_area = 6,678.63 m² | opening_date = April 17, 2004 (Heisei 16) | closing_date = | postal_code = 881-0005 | address = Miyake Aza Saitobaru Nishi 5670, Saito City, Miyazaki Prefecture | latitude_deg = 32 | latitude_min = 07 | latitude_sec = 31.3 | longitude_deg = 131 | longitude_min = 22 | longitude_sec = 56.6 | access = | website = [http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/index.html Official website] }}
'''Miyazaki Prefectural Saitobaru Archaeological Museum''' (宮崎県立西都原考古博物館, ''Miyazaki Kenritsu Saitobaru Kōko Hakubutsukan'') is a public [[:en:Archaeological museum|Archaeological museum]] located in Miyake Aza Saitobaru Nishi, [[Saito City]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]], Japan. Situated near the [[:en:Special Historic Site|Special Historic Site]]-designated [[Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]], the museum primarily exhibits materials related to the kofun group while hosting events on [[:en:Archaeology|Archaeology]] and [[historical sites]] throughout Miyazaki Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The Saitobaru Kofun Group, located in central Miyazaki on the Saitobaru Plateau, is a massive collection of 319 [[kofun]] (Kofuns), including the imperial tomb references [[Ozasahozuka Kofun|Osahozuka Kofun]] and [[Mesahozuka Kofun]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=Overview of the Saitobaru Kofun Group|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/guidance.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>. Opened on April 17, 2004, the Saitobaru Archaeological Museum was established to house, preserve, and display archaeological materials from the kofun group. The museum also explores various archaeological themes from the [[Kofun period]] and beyond<ref>{{Cite web|title=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Exhibition Archives|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/exhibition.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>.
=== Features ===
In addition to exhibits covering the Kofun period across the [[Japanese archipelago]], the museum showcases unique burial practices from [[:en:Southern Kyushu|Southern Kyushu]], such as [[underground horizontal chamber tombs]] specific to the Saitobaru Kofun Group. Other exhibits delve into the [[:en:Hayato people|Hayato people]] of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka]] and [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]s, who were considered [[:en:barbarians|barbarians]] under the [[Ritsuryō system]].
The museum conducts periodic special exhibitions, archaeology lectures, workshops, and experiential learning events. A unique "ever-changing exhibition" concept replaces the traditional idea of "permanent exhibitions," ensuring new information is continuously displayed<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Kitagō|first=Yasumichi|year=2004|title=Abolishing Permanent Exhibitions|journal=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Column|volume=7|pages=1-2|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/collumn/007.pdf|format=PDF|ref=harv}}</ref>.
Universal design principles enhance accessibility, including tactile signs and displays for visitors with visual or hearing impairments. The "hands-on exhibits" allow direct interaction with artifacts to promote experiential learning<ref>{{Cite web|title=Universal Design at the Saitobaru Archaeological Museum|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/universal.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>. The exhibit areas are intentionally designed without fixed routes or pathways.
== Facilities == [[File:Saitobaru-kofungun_yokoana-bogun.JPG|thumb|280px|Sakemotonoue Underground Chamber Tomb Group Preservation Building (2013)]] [[File:Saitobaru_Konohanakan.jpg|thumb|280px|Konohana Hall (2019)]] * Ancient Living Experience Center * Saitobaru Guidance Center "Konohana Hall" * Sakemotonoue Underground Chamber Tomb Preservation Building * Saitobaru No.111 Tomb and No.4 [[Underground Horizontal Chamber Tomb]] Preservation Facility
== Visitor Information == * Admission: Free * Opening Hours: 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM * Closed: Mondays, the day following public holidays, and year-end/New Year holidays (December 28 to
January 4) * Parking: 100 spaces for regular vehicles, 8 for large buses, and 8 wheelchair-accessible spaces<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Visitor Information|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/usage.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>
== Access == * **Bus**: From [[:en:Miyako City|Miyako City]], board the "Saito Bus Center (via Saitobaru Archaeological Museum)" route (approximately 70 minutes). Alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." * **Car**: Approximately 40 minutes from [[:en:Miyazaki City|Miyazaki City]] via [[:en:National Route 219|National Route 219]] or 10 minutes from the [[Nishitō Interchange]] on the [[:en:Higashikyushu Expressway|Higashikyushu Expressway]]<ref name=":0" />.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Saitobaru Archaeological Museum of Miyazaki Prefecture]][[ja:宮崎県立西都原考古博物館]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Saitobaru Archaeological Museum of Miyazaki Prefecture]][[ja:宮崎県立西都原考古博物館]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Saitobaru Archaeological Museum of Miyazaki Prefecture]][[ja:宮崎県立西都原考古博物館]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q68478310]] Wikidata name: Saitobaru Archaeological Museum of Miyazaki Prefecture [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Museum | Name = Miyazaki Prefectural Saitobaru Archaeological Museum | native_name = The Saitobaru Archaeological Museum of Miyazaki Prefecture | native_name_lang = en | imagesize = 280px | image = File:Saitobaru Archaeology Museum.jpg | image_caption = Front entrance | official_name = 宮崎県立西都原考古博物館 | alias = | predecessor = Saitobaru Resource Center | field = History, Archaeology, Saitobaru Kofun Group | collection_size = | visitors = | director = | curators = | research_staff = | operated_by = [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]] | management = | annual_budget = | building_design = Reinforced concrete, 4 stories (1 basement, 3 above ground) | total_floor_area = 6,678.63 m² | opening_date = April 17, 2004 (Heisei 16) | closing_date = | postal_code = 881-0005 | address = Miyake Aza Saitobaru Nishi 5670, Saito City, Miyazaki Prefecture | latitude_deg = 32 | latitude_min = 07 | latitude_sec = 31.3 | longitude_deg = 131 | longitude_min = 22 | longitude_sec = 56.6 | access = | website = [http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/index.html Official website] }}
'''Miyazaki Prefectural Saitobaru Archaeological Museum''' (宮崎県立西都原考古博物館, ''Miyazaki Kenritsu Saitobaru Kōko Hakubutsukan'') is a public [[:en:Archaeological museum|Archaeological museum]] located in Miyake Aza Saitobaru Nishi, [[Saito City]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]], Japan. Situated near the [[:en:Special Historic Site|Special Historic Site]]-designated [[Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]], the museum primarily exhibits materials related to the kofun group while hosting events on [[:en:Archaeology|Archaeology]] and [[historical sites]] throughout Miyazaki Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The Saitobaru Kofun Group, located in central Miyazaki on the Saitobaru Plateau, is a massive collection of 319 [[kofun]] (Kofuns), including the imperial tomb references [[Ozasahozuka Kofun|Osahozuka Kofun]] and [[Mesahozuka Kofun]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=Overview of the Saitobaru Kofun Group|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/guidance.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>. Opened on April 17, 2004, the Saitobaru Archaeological Museum was established to house, preserve, and display archaeological materials from the kofun group. The museum also explores various archaeological themes from the [[Kofun period]] and beyond<ref>{{Cite web|title=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Exhibition Archives|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/exhibition.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>.
=== Features ===
In addition to exhibits covering the Kofun period across the [[Japanese archipelago]], the museum showcases unique burial practices from [[:en:Southern Kyushu|Southern Kyushu]], such as [[underground horizontal chamber tombs]] specific to the Saitobaru Kofun Group. Other exhibits delve into the [[:en:Hayato people|Hayato people]] of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka]] and [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]s, who were considered [[:en:barbarians|barbarians]] under the [[Ritsuryō system]].
The museum conducts periodic special exhibitions, archaeology lectures, workshops, and experiential learning events. A unique "ever-changing exhibition" concept replaces the traditional idea of "permanent exhibitions," ensuring new information is continuously displayed<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Kitagō|first=Yasumichi|year=2004|title=Abolishing Permanent Exhibitions|journal=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Column|volume=7|pages=1-2|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/collumn/007.pdf|format=PDF|ref=harv}}</ref>.
Universal design principles enhance accessibility, including tactile signs and displays for visitors with visual or hearing impairments. The "hands-on exhibits" allow direct interaction with artifacts to promote experiential learning<ref>{{Cite web|title=Universal Design at the Saitobaru Archaeological Museum|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/universal.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>. The exhibit areas are intentionally designed without fixed routes or pathways.
== Facilities == [[File:Saitobaru-kofungun_yokoana-bogun.JPG|thumb|280px|Sakemotonoue Underground Chamber Tomb Group Preservation Building (2013)]] [[File:Saitobaru_Konohanakan.jpg|thumb|280px|Konohana Hall (2019)]] * Ancient Living Experience Center * Saitobaru Guidance Center "Konohana Hall" * Sakemotonoue Underground Chamber Tomb Preservation Building * Saitobaru No.111 Tomb and No.4 [[Underground Horizontal Chamber Tomb]] Preservation Facility
== Visitor Information == * Admission: Free * Opening Hours: 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM * Closed: Mondays, the day following public holidays, and year-end/New Year holidays (December 28 to
January 4) * Parking: 100 spaces for regular vehicles, 8 for large buses, and 8 wheelchair-accessible spaces<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Visitor Information|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/usage.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>
== Access == * **Bus**: From [[:en:Miyako City|Miyako City]], board the "Saito Bus Center (via Saitobaru Archaeological Museum)" route (approximately 70 minutes). Alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." * **Car**: Approximately 40 minutes from [[:en:Miyazaki City|Miyazaki City]] via [[:en:National Route 219|National Route 219]] or 10 minutes from the [[Nishitō Interchange]] on the [[:en:Higashikyushu Expressway|Higashikyushu Expressway]]<ref name=":0" />.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Citation help}}
== Related Articles == * [[:en:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]]
== External Links == * [https://www.saito-kanko.jp/sightseeing/%e5%ae%ae%e5%b4%8e%e7%9c%8c%e7%ab%8b%e8%a5%bf%e9%83%bd%e5%8e%9f%e8%80%83%e5%8f%a4%e5%8d%9a%e7%89%a9%e9%a4%a8 Saito City Tourism Association] * [http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/index.html Official website]
{{Museum Law Institutions}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Saitobaru Archaeological Museum of Miyazaki Prefecture]][[ja:宮崎県立西都原考古博物館]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Saitobaru Archaeological Museum of Miyazaki Prefecture]][[ja:宮崎県立西都原考古博物館]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Saitobaru Archaeological Museum of Miyazaki Prefecture]][[ja:宮崎県立西都原考古博物館]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q68478310]] Wikidata name: Saitobaru Archaeological Museum of Miyazaki Prefecture [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Museum | Name = Miyazaki Prefectural Saitobaru Archaeological Museum | native_name = The Saitobaru Archaeological Museum of Miyazaki Prefecture | native_name_lang = en | imagesize = 280px | image = File:Saitobaru Archaeology Museum.jpg | image_caption = Front entrance | official_name = 宮崎県立西都原考古博物館 | alias = | predecessor = Saitobaru Resource Center | field = History, Archaeology, Saitobaru Kofun Group | collection_size = | visitors = | director = | curators = | research_staff = | operated_by = {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|WD=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture}} | management = | annual_budget = | building_design = Reinforced concrete, 4 stories (1 basement, 3 above ground) | total_floor_area = 6,678.63 m² | opening_date = April 17, 2004 (Heisei 16) | closing_date = | postal_code = 881-0005 | address = Miyake Aza Saitobaru Nishi 5670, Saito City, Miyazaki Prefecture | latitude_deg = 32 | latitude_min = 07 | latitude_sec = 31.3 | longitude_deg = 131 | longitude_min = 22 | longitude_sec = 56.6 | access = | website = [http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/index.html Official website] }}
'''Miyazaki Prefectural Saitobaru Archaeological Museum''' (宮崎県立西都原考古博物館, ''Miyazaki Kenritsu Saitobaru Kōko Hakubutsukan'') is a public [[:en:Archaeological museum|Archaeological museum]] located in Miyake Aza Saitobaru Nishi, [[Saito City]], {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|WD=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture}}, Japan. Situated near the {{ill|Special Historic Site|en|Special Historic Site|WD=Q26764449|lt=Special Historic Site}}-designated [[Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]], the museum primarily exhibits materials related to the kofun group while hosting events on {{ill|Archaeology|en|Archaeology|WD=Q23498|lt=Archaeology}} and [[historical sites]] throughout Miyazaki Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The Saitobaru Kofun Group, located in central Miyazaki on the Saitobaru Plateau, is a massive collection of 319 [[kofun]] (Kofuns), including the imperial tomb references [[Ozasahozuka Kofun|Osahozuka Kofun]] and [[Mesahozuka Kofun]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=Overview of the Saitobaru Kofun Group|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/guidance.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>. Opened on April 17, 2004, the Saitobaru Archaeological Museum was established to house, preserve, and display archaeological materials from the kofun group. The museum also explores various archaeological themes from the [[Kofun period]] and beyond<ref>{{Cite web|title=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Exhibition Archives|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/exhibition.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>.
=== Features ===
In addition to exhibits covering the Kofun period across the [[Japanese archipelago]], the museum showcases unique burial practices from {{ill|Southern Kyushu|en|Southern Kyushu|WD=Q8017707|lt=Southern Kyushu}}, such as [[underground horizontal chamber tombs]] specific to the Saitobaru Kofun Group. Other exhibits delve into the {{ill|Hayato people|en|Hayato people|WD=Q1151764|lt=Hayato people}} of the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka}} and {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}}s, who were considered [[:en:barbarians|barbarians]] under the [[Ritsuryō system]].
The museum conducts periodic special exhibitions, archaeology lectures, workshops, and experiential learning events. A unique "ever-changing exhibition" concept replaces the traditional idea of "permanent exhibitions," ensuring new information is continuously displayed<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Kitagō|first=Yasumichi|year=2004|title=Abolishing Permanent Exhibitions|journal=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Column|volume=7|pages=1-2|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/collumn/007.pdf|format=PDF|ref=harv}}</ref>.
Universal design principles enhance accessibility, including tactile signs and displays for visitors with visual or hearing impairments. The "hands-on exhibits" allow direct interaction with artifacts to promote experiential learning<ref>{{Cite web|title=Universal Design at the Saitobaru Archaeological Museum|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/universal.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>. The exhibit areas are intentionally designed without fixed routes or pathways.
== Facilities == [[File:Saitobaru-kofungun_yokoana-bogun.JPG|thumb|280px|Sakemotonoue Underground Chamber Tomb Group Preservation Building (2013)]] [[File:Saitobaru_Konohanakan.jpg|thumb|280px|Konohana Hall (2019)]] * Ancient Living Experience Center * Saitobaru Guidance Center "Konohana Hall" * Sakemotonoue Underground Chamber Tomb Preservation Building * Saitobaru No.111 Tomb and No.4 [[Underground Horizontal Chamber Tomb]] Preservation Facility
== Visitor Information == * Admission: Free * Opening Hours: 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM * Closed: Mondays, the day following public holidays, and year-end/New Year holidays (December 28 to
January 4) * Parking: 100 spaces for regular vehicles, 8 for large buses, and 8 wheelchair-accessible spaces<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Visitor Information|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/usage.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>
== Access == * **Bus**: From [[:en:Miyako City|Miyako City]], board the "Saito Bus Center (via Saitobaru Archaeological Museum)" route (approximately 70 minutes). Alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." * **Car**: Approximately 40 minutes from [[:en:Miyazaki City|Miyazaki City]] via [[:en:National Route 219|National Route 219]] or 10 minutes from the [[Nishitō Interchange]] on the {{ill|Higashikyushu Expressway|en|Higashikyushu Expressway|WD=Q2648028|lt=Higashikyushu Expressway}}<ref name=":0" />.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Citation help}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|en|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|WD=Q3469314|lt=Saitobaru Kofun Group}}
== External Links == * [https://www.saito-kanko.jp/sightseeing/%e5%ae%ae%e5%b4%8e%e7%9c%8c%e7%ab%8b%e8%a5%bf%e9%83%bd%e5%8e%9f%e8%80%83%e5%8f%a4%e5%8d%9a%e7%89%a9%e9%a4%a8 Saito City Tourism Association] * [http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/index.html Official website]

{{Museum | Name = Miyazaki Prefectural Saitobaru Archaeological Museum | native_name = The Saitobaru Archaeological Museum of Miyazaki Prefecture | native_name_lang = en | imagesize = 280px | image = File:Saitobaru Archaeology Museum.jpg | image_caption = Front entrance | official_name = 宮崎県立西都原考古博物館 | alias = | predecessor = Saitobaru Resource Center | field = History, Archaeology, Saitobaru Kofun Group | collection_size = | visitors = | director = | curators = | research_staff = | operated_by = {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|WD=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture}} | management = | annual_budget = | building_design = Reinforced concrete, 4 stories (1 basement, 3 above ground) | total_floor_area = 6,678.63 m² | opening_date = April 17, 2004 (Heisei 16) | closing_date = | postal_code = 881-0005 | address = Miyake Aza Saitobaru Nishi 5670, Saito City, Miyazaki Prefecture | latitude_deg = 32 | latitude_min = 07 | latitude_sec = 31.3 | longitude_deg = 131 | longitude_min = 22 | longitude_sec = 56.6 | access = | website = [http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/index.html Official website] }}
'''Miyazaki Prefectural Saitobaru Archaeological Museum''' (宮崎県立西都原考古博物館, ''Miyazaki Kenritsu Saitobaru Kōko Hakubutsukan'') is a public [[:en:Archaeological museum|Archaeological museum]] located in Miyake Aza Saitobaru Nishi, [[Saito City]], {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|WD=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture}}, Japan. Situated near the {{ill|Special Historic Site|en|Special Historic Site|WD=Q26764449|lt=Special Historic Site}}-designated [[Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]], the museum primarily exhibits materials related to the kofun group while hosting events on {{ill|Archaeology|en|Archaeology|WD=Q23498|lt=Archaeology}} and [[historical sites]] throughout Miyazaki Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The Saitobaru Kofun Group, located in central Miyazaki on the Saitobaru Plateau, is a massive collection of 319 [[kofun]] (Kofuns), including the imperial tomb references [[Ozasahozuka Kofun|Osahozuka Kofun]] and [[Mesahozuka Kofun]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=Overview of the Saitobaru Kofun Group|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/guidance.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>. Opened on April 17, 2004, the Saitobaru Archaeological Museum was established to house, preserve, and display archaeological materials from the kofun group. The museum also explores various archaeological themes from the [[Kofun period]] and beyond<ref>{{Cite web|title=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Exhibition Archives|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/exhibition.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>.
=== Features ===
In addition to exhibits covering the Kofun period across the [[Japanese archipelago]], the museum showcases unique burial practices from {{ill|Southern Kyushu|en|Southern Kyushu|WD=Q8017707|lt=Southern Kyushu}}, such as [[underground horizontal chamber tombs]] specific to the Saitobaru Kofun Group. Other exhibits delve into the {{ill|Hayato people|en|Hayato people|WD=Q1151764|lt=Hayato people}} of the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka}} and {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}}s, who were considered [[:en:barbarians|barbarians]] under the [[Ritsuryō system]].
The museum conducts periodic special exhibitions, archaeology lectures, workshops, and experiential learning events. A unique "ever-changing exhibition" concept replaces the traditional idea of "permanent exhibitions," ensuring new information is continuously displayed<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Kitagō|first=Yasumichi|year=2004|title=Abolishing Permanent Exhibitions|journal=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Column|volume=7|pages=1-2|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/collumn/007.pdf|format=PDF|ref=harv}}</ref>.
Universal design principles enhance accessibility, including tactile signs and displays for visitors with visual or hearing impairments. The "hands-on exhibits" allow direct interaction with artifacts to promote experiential learning<ref>{{Cite web|title=Universal Design at the Saitobaru Archaeological Museum|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/universal.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>. The exhibit areas are intentionally designed without fixed routes or pathways.
== Facilities == [[File:Saitobaru-kofungun_yokoana-bogun.JPG|thumb|280px|Sakemotonoue Underground Chamber Tomb Group Preservation Building (2013)]] [[File:Saitobaru_Konohanakan.jpg|thumb|280px|Konohana Hall (2019)]] * Ancient Living Experience Center * Saitobaru Guidance Center "Konohana Hall" * Sakemotonoue Underground Chamber Tomb Preservation Building * Saitobaru No.111 Tomb and No.4 [[Underground Horizontal Chamber Tomb]] Preservation Facility
== Visitor Information == * Admission: Free * Opening Hours: 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM * Closed: Mondays, the day following public holidays, and year-end/New Year holidays (December 28 to
January 4) * Parking: 100 spaces for regular vehicles, 8 for large buses, and 8 wheelchair-accessible spaces<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Visitor Information|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/usage.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>
== Access == * **Bus**: From [[:en:Miyako City|Miyako City]], board the "Saito Bus Center (via Saitobaru Archaeological Museum)" route (approximately 70 minutes). Alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." * **Car**: Approximately 40 minutes from [[:en:Miyazaki City|Miyazaki City]] via [[:en:National Route 219|National Route 219]] or 10 minutes from the [[Nishitō Interchange]] on the {{ill|Higashikyushu Expressway|en|Higashikyushu Expressway|WD=Q2648028|lt=Higashikyushu Expressway}}<ref name=":0" />.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Citation help}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|en|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|WD=Q3469314|lt=Saitobaru Kofun Group}}
== External Links == * [https://www.saito-kanko.jp/sightseeing/%e5%ae%ae%e5%b4%8e%e7%9c%8c%e7%ab%8b%e8%a5%bf%e9%83%bd%e5%8e%9f%e8%80%83%e5%8f%a4%e5%8d%9a%e7%89%a9%e9%a4%a8 Saito City Tourism Association] * [http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/index.html Official website]
{{Museum Law Institutions}}

[[ja:宮崎県立西都原考古博物館]]

{{Museum | Name = Miyazaki Prefectural Saitobaru Archaeological Museum | native_name = The Saitobaru Archaeological Museum of Miyazaki Prefecture | native_name_lang = en | imagesize = 280px | image = File:Saitobaru Archaeology Museum.jpg | image_caption = Front entrance | official_name = 宮崎県立西都原考古博物館 | alias = | predecessor = Saitobaru Resource Center | field = History, Archaeology, Saitobaru Kofun Group | collection_size = | visitors = | director = | curators = | research_staff = | operated_by = {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|WD=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture|1=Miyazaki Prefecture}} | management = | annual_budget = | building_design = Reinforced concrete, 4 stories (1 basement, 3 above ground) | total_floor_area = 6,678.63 m² | opening_date = April 17, 2004 (Heisei 16) | closing_date = | postal_code = 881-0005 | address = Miyake Aza Saitobaru Nishi 5670, Saito City, Miyazaki Prefecture | latitude_deg = 32 | latitude_min = 07 | latitude_sec = 31.3 | longitude_deg = 131 | longitude_min = 22 | longitude_sec = 56.6 | access = | website = [http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/index.html Official website] }}
'''Miyazaki Prefectural Saitobaru Archaeological Museum''' (宮崎県立西都原考古博物館, ''Miyazaki Kenritsu Saitobaru Kōko Hakubutsukan'') is a public [[:en:Archaeological museum|Archaeological museum]] located in Miyake Aza Saitobaru Nishi, [[Saito City]], {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|WD=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture|1=Miyazaki Prefecture}}, Japan. Situated near the {{ill|Special Historic Site|en|Special Historic Site|WD=Q26764449|lt=Special Historic Site|1=Special Historic Site}}-designated [[Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]], the museum primarily exhibits materials related to the kofun group while hosting events on {{ill|Archaeology|en|Archaeology|WD=Q23498|lt=Archaeology|1=Archaeology}} and [[historical sites]] throughout Miyazaki Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The Saitobaru Kofun Group, located in central Miyazaki on the Saitobaru Plateau, is a massive collection of 319 [[kofun]] (Kofuns), including the imperial tomb references [[Ozasahozuka Kofun|Osahozuka Kofun]] and [[Mesahozuka Kofun]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=Overview of the Saitobaru Kofun Group|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/guidance.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>. Opened on April 17, 2004, the Saitobaru Archaeological Museum was established to house, preserve, and display archaeological materials from the kofun group. The museum also explores various archaeological themes from the [[Kofun period]] and beyond<ref>{{Cite web|title=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Exhibition Archives|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/exhibition.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>.
=== Features ===
In addition to exhibits covering the Kofun period across the [[Japanese archipelago]], the museum showcases unique burial practices from {{ill|Southern Kyushu|en|Southern Kyushu|WD=Q8017707|lt=Southern Kyushu|1=Southern Kyushu}}, such as [[underground horizontal chamber tombs]] specific to the Saitobaru Kofun Group. Other exhibits delve into the {{ill|Hayato people|en|Hayato people|WD=Q1151764|lt=Hayato people|1=Hayato people}} of the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka|1=Asuka period}} and {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}s, who were considered [[:en:barbarians|barbarians]] under the [[Ritsuryō system]].
The museum conducts periodic special exhibitions, archaeology lectures, workshops, and experiential learning events. A unique "ever-changing exhibition" concept replaces the traditional idea of "permanent exhibitions," ensuring new information is continuously displayed<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Kitagō|first=Yasumichi|year=2004|title=Abolishing Permanent Exhibitions|journal=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Column|volume=7|pages=1-2|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/collumn/007.pdf|format=PDF|ref=harv}}</ref>.
Universal design principles enhance accessibility, including tactile signs and displays for visitors with visual or hearing impairments. The "hands-on exhibits" allow direct interaction with artifacts to promote experiential learning<ref>{{Cite web|title=Universal Design at the Saitobaru Archaeological Museum|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/universal.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>. The exhibit areas are intentionally designed without fixed routes or pathways.
== Facilities == [[File:Saitobaru-kofungun_yokoana-bogun.JPG|thumb|280px|Sakemotonoue Underground Chamber Tomb Group Preservation Building (2013)]] [[File:Saitobaru_Konohanakan.jpg|thumb|280px|Konohana Hall (2019)]] * Ancient Living Experience Center * Saitobaru Guidance Center "Konohana Hall" * Sakemotonoue Underground Chamber Tomb Preservation Building * Saitobaru No.111 Tomb and No.4 [[Underground Horizontal Chamber Tomb]] Preservation Facility
== Visitor Information == * Admission: Free * Opening Hours: 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM * Closed: Mondays, the day following public holidays, and year-end/New Year holidays (December 28 to
January 4) * Parking: 100 spaces for regular vehicles, 8 for large buses, and 8 wheelchair-accessible spaces<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Visitor Information|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/usage.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>
== Access == * **Bus**: From [[:en:Miyako City|Miyako City]], board the "Saito Bus Center (via Saitobaru Archaeological Museum)" route (approximately 70 minutes). Alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." * **Car**: Approximately 40 minutes from [[:en:Miyazaki City|Miyazaki City]] via [[:en:National Route 219|National Route 219]] or 10 minutes from the [[Nishitō Interchange]] on the {{ill|Higashikyushu Expressway|en|Higashikyushu Expressway|WD=Q2648028|lt=Higashikyushu Expressway|1=Higashikyushu Expressway}}<ref name=":0" />.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Citation help}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|en|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|WD=Q3469314|lt=Saitobaru Kofun Group|1=Saitobaru Kofun Cluster}}
== External Links == * [https://www.saito-kanko.jp/sightseeing/%e5%ae%ae%e5%b4%8e%e7%9c%8c%e7%ab%8b%e8%a5%bf%e9%83%bd%e5%8e%9f%e8%80%83%e5%8f%a4%e5%8d%9a%e7%89%a9%e9%a4%a8 Saito City Tourism Association] * [http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/index.html Official website]
{{Museum Law Institutions}}

{{Museum | Name = Miyazaki Prefectural Saitobaru Archaeological Museum | native_name = The Saitobaru Archaeological Museum of Miyazaki Prefecture | native_name_lang = en | imagesize = 280px | image = File:Saitobaru Archaeology Museum.jpg | image_caption = Front entrance | official_name = 宮崎県立西都原考古博物館 | alias = | predecessor = Saitobaru Resource Center | field = History, Archaeology, Saitobaru Kofun Group | collection_size = | visitors = | director = | curators = | research_staff = | operated_by = {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|qid=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture|1=Miyazaki Prefecture}} | management = | annual_budget = | building_design = Reinforced concrete, 4 stories (1 basement, 3 above ground) | total_floor_area = 6,678.63 m² | opening_date = April 17, 2004 (Heisei 16) | closing_date = | postal_code = 881-0005 | address = Miyake Aza Saitobaru Nishi 5670, Saito City, Miyazaki Prefecture | latitude_deg = 32 | latitude_min = 07 | latitude_sec = 31.3 | longitude_deg = 131 | longitude_min = 22 | longitude_sec = 56.6 | access = | website = [http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/index.html Official website] }}
'''Miyazaki Prefectural Saitobaru Archaeological Museum''' (宮崎県立西都原考古博物館, ''Miyazaki Kenritsu Saitobaru Kōko Hakubutsukan'') is a public [[:en:Archaeological museum|Archaeological museum]] located in Miyake Aza Saitobaru Nishi, [[Saito City]], {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|qid=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture|1=Miyazaki Prefecture}}, Japan. Situated near the {{ill|Special Historic Site|en|Special Historic Site|qid=Q26764449|lt=Special Historic Site|1=Special Historic Site}}-designated [[Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]], the museum primarily exhibits materials related to the kofun group while hosting events on {{ill|Archaeology|en|Archaeology|qid=Q23498|lt=Archaeology|1=Archaeology}} and [[historical sites]] throughout Miyazaki Prefecture.
== Overview ==
The Saitobaru Kofun Group, located in central Miyazaki on the Saitobaru Plateau, is a massive collection of 319 [[kofun]] (Kofuns), including the imperial tomb references [[Ozasahozuka Kofun|Osahozuka Kofun]] and [[Mesahozuka Kofun]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=Overview of the Saitobaru Kofun Group|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/guidance.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>. Opened on April 17, 2004, the Saitobaru Archaeological Museum was established to house, preserve, and display archaeological materials from the kofun group. The museum also explores various archaeological themes from the [[Kofun period]] and beyond<ref>{{Cite web|title=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Exhibition Archives|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/exhibition.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>.
=== Features ===
In addition to exhibits covering the Kofun period across the [[Japanese archipelago]], the museum showcases unique burial practices from {{ill|Southern Kyushu|en|Southern Kyushu|qid=Q8017707|lt=Southern Kyushu|1=Southern Kyushu}}, such as [[underground horizontal chamber tombs]] specific to the Saitobaru Kofun Group. Other exhibits delve into the {{ill|Hayato people|en|Hayato people|qid=Q1151764|lt=Hayato people|1=Hayato people}} of the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|qid=Q210688|lt=Asuka|1=Asuka period}} and {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|qid=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}s, who were considered [[:en:barbarians|barbarians]] under the [[Ritsuryō system]].
The museum conducts periodic special exhibitions, archaeology lectures, workshops, and experiential learning events. A unique "ever-changing exhibition" concept replaces the traditional idea of "permanent exhibitions," ensuring new information is continuously displayed<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Kitagō|first=Yasumichi|year=2004|title=Abolishing Permanent Exhibitions|journal=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Column|volume=7|pages=1-2|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/collumn/007.pdf|format=PDF|ref=harv}}</ref>.
Universal design principles enhance accessibility, including tactile signs and displays for visitors with visual or hearing impairments. The "hands-on exhibits" allow direct interaction with artifacts to promote experiential learning<ref>{{Cite web|title=Universal Design at the Saitobaru Archaeological Museum|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/universal.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>. The exhibit areas are intentionally designed without fixed routes or pathways.
== Facilities == [[File:Saitobaru-kofungun_yokoana-bogun.JPG|thumb|280px|Sakemotonoue Underground Chamber Tomb Group Preservation Building (2013)]] [[File:Saitobaru_Konohanakan.jpg|thumb|280px|Konohana Hall (2019)]] * Ancient Living Experience Center * Saitobaru Guidance Center "Konohana Hall" * Sakemotonoue Underground Chamber Tomb Preservation Building * Saitobaru No.111 Tomb and No.4 [[Underground Horizontal Chamber Tomb]] Preservation Facility
== Visitor Information == * Admission: Free * Opening Hours: 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM * Closed: Mondays, the day following public holidays, and year-end/New Year holidays (December 28 to
January 4) * Parking: 100 spaces for regular vehicles, 8 for large buses, and 8 wheelchair-accessible spaces<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Saitobaru Archaeological Museum Visitor Information|url=http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/usage.html|website=saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp|accessdate=2019-09-24}}</ref>
== Access == * **Bus**: From [[:en:Miyako City|Miyako City]], board the "Saito Bus Center (via Saitobaru Archaeological Museum)" route (approximately 70 minutes). Alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum." * **Car**: Approximately 40 minutes from [[:en:Miyazaki City|Miyazaki City]] via [[:en:National Route 219|National Route 219]] or 10 minutes from the [[Nishitō Interchange]] on the {{ill|Higashikyushu Expressway|en|Higashikyushu Expressway|qid=Q2648028|lt=Higashikyushu Expressway|1=Higashikyushu Expressway}}<ref name=":0" />.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Citation help}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|en|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|qid=Q3469314|lt=Saitobaru Kofun Group|1=Saitobaru Kofun Cluster}}
== External Links == * [https://www.saito-kanko.jp/sightseeing/%e5%ae%ae%e5%b4%8e%e7%9c%8c%e7%ab%8b%e8%a5%bf%e9%83%bd%e5%8e%9f%e8%80%83%e5%8f%a4%e5%8d%9a%e7%89%a9%e9%a4%a8 Saito City Tourism Association] * [http://saito-muse.pref.miyazaki.jp/web/index.html Official website]
{{Museum Law Institutions}}

[[ja:宮崎県立西都原考古博物館]]
